name: Creamy Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil and add 1 tablespoon of sea salt.
Add the penne and cook for 10-12 minutes until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup of starchy pasta water before draining.
In a wide skillet, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant but not browned.
Lower the heat and pour in the heavy cream. Let it simmer gently for 3 minutes until it starts to thicken slightly.
Whisk in the grated parmesan and a pinch of nutmeg. Stir constantly until the cheese is fully melted and the sauce is silky.
Add the drained pasta to the skillet. Toss vigorously, adding splashes of the reserved pasta water to reach a glossy, coating consistency.
Season with fresh black pepper and garnish with chopped parsley before serving.

name: Tomato Pasta
steps: Boil the spaghetti in heavily salted water. Aim for 1 minute less than the package instructions for a perfect al dente finish.
Heat olive oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Add the onion and sauté until translucent (about 5 minutes).
Add the smashed garlic and chili flakes. Cook for 2 minutes to infuse the oil, then remove the garlic cloves if a milder flavor is desired.
Pour in the canned tomatoes. Use a wooden spoon to crush the whole tomatoes into the sauce.
Simmer the sauce on low heat for 15 minutes. Stir in half of the basil leaves (torn by hand) halfway through.
Drain the pasta and add it directly to the sauce. Increase the heat to high and toss for 60 seconds so the pasta absorbs the tomato flavor.
Serve with a drizzle of fresh olive oil and the remaining basil leaves on top.

name: Pesto Pasta
steps: Toast the pine nuts in a dry pan over medium heat for 2-3 minutes until golden and fragrant. Let them cool.
In a food processor, pulse the garlic and toasted pine nuts until they form a coarse paste.
Add the fresh basil leaves and a pinch of sea salt. Pulse in short bursts to avoid heating the basil (which turns it bitter and brown).
Slowly drizzle in the olive oil while pulsing until the mixture is creamy but still has some texture.
Transfer the pesto to a large bowl and stir in the grated cheeses by hand.
Boil the pasta until al dente. Reserve 1/4 cup of pasta water.
Toss the hot pasta with the pesto in the large bowl (never heat pesto in a pan). Add pasta water gradually to loosen the sauce until it coats the pasta perfectly.

name: Garlic Oil Pasta
steps: Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of salted water. It should be slightly undercooked as it will finish in the pan.
While the pasta boils, place the olive oil and sliced garlic in a cold skillet.
Turn the heat to medium-low. This allows the garlic to soften and infuse the oil slowly without burning.
When the garlic turns pale golden, add the chili flakes and 2 tablespoons of the boiling pasta water. This creates an emulsion.
Transfer the spaghetti directly from the water to the skillet using tongs.
Add the chopped parsley and toss vigorously over medium heat for 1 minute until the oil is thick and coats every strand.
Finish with a tiny squeeze of lemon juice for brightness and serve immediately.

name: Spaghetti Meat Sauce
steps: In a large heavy-bottomed pot, brown the ground beef over high heat. Drain about half of the excess fat.
Reduce heat to medium. Add the onion, carrot, and celery (soffritto). Cook for 8 minutes until softened.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Pour in the red wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up the browned bits.
Add the tomato passata, Italian herbs, salt, and a pinch of black pepper. Bring to a light boil.
Reduce heat to very low, cover partially, and simmer for at least 45 minutes. The sauce should be thick and rich.
Cook the spaghetti in salted water until al dente.
Combine the pasta with the meat sauce and serve with plenty of grated parmesan.

name: Lasagna Slice
steps: Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F).
In a bowl, mix the ricotta, egg, half of the parmesan, and chopped parsley until smooth.
Spread a thin layer of meat sauce on the bottom of a 9x9 inch baking dish.
Layer: pasta sheets, a thin layer of ricotta mixture, meat sauce, and a handful of mozzarella.
Repeat layers until all ingredients are used, ensuring the top layer is pasta covered generously with meat sauce and the remaining mozzarella/parmesan.
Cover with foil (don't let it touch the cheese) and bake for 25 minutes.
Remove foil and bake for another 20 minutes until the cheese is bubbling and golden. Let it rest for 15 minutes before slicing to ensure clean square layers.

name: Baked Pasta
steps: Boil the pasta in salted water for 2 minutes less than the package instructions (it will finish cooking in the oven).
In a large bowl, combine the par-cooked pasta, marinara sauce, and ricotta cheese. Stir gently until the pasta is coated in the pinkish sauce.
Transfer half of the mixture to a large baking dish. Sprinkle with half of the mozzarella.
Top with the remaining pasta mixture and the rest of the mozzarella and parmesan.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 20-25 minutes until the top is browned and crispy in spots.
Garnish with fresh basil leaves before serving.

name: Penne Pasta
steps: Fill a large pot with 3-4 liters of water and bring to a rolling boil.
Add 1 generous tablespoon of sea salt. The water should taste like the sea.
Add the penne and stir immediately to prevent the tubes from sticking to each other or the bottom.
Maintain a steady boil. Stir occasionally.
At the 10-minute mark, taste a piece. It should be firm to the bite (al dente).
Drain the pasta immediately. If not using sauce right away, toss with 1 tsp of olive oil to prevent clumping.

name: Fettuccine Pasta
steps: Use a tall pot filled with plenty of salted water. Bring to a boil.
Gently place the fettuccine nests into the water. Wait 30 seconds for them to soften, then use tongs to carefully untangle the ribbons.
Cook for 9-11 minutes. Stir frequently as long flat pasta tends to stick together easily.
Test a strand for the perfect al dente texture.
Drain and toss with a small knob of butter. This keeps the ribbons separate and ready for creamy Alfredo sauce.

name: Rigatoni Pasta
steps: Rigatoni requires more water than smaller shapes; use a large pot and bring to a boil.
Add salt and the pasta. Stir for the first 2 minutes.
Rigatoni has a longer cooking time (approx. 12-14 mins). The large holes and ridges make it perfect for chunky meat or vegetable sauces.
Drain and do not rinse. The starch on the surface helps thick sauces adhere to the ridges.

name: Linguine Pasta
steps: Bring a large pot of water to a boil and add salt.
Add the linguine. Use a pasta fork to push the strands down as they soften.
Cook for 9-11 minutes. Linguine is thinner than fettuccine and cooks slightly faster.
Drain and serve. This shape is the classic choice for clam sauce or shrimp scampi.

name: Ravioli / Stuffed Pasta
steps: Bring a wide, shallow pan of water to a gentle simmer. Do not use a hard boil as it can break the delicate pasta shells.
Add salt and a drop of olive oil to the water.
Carefully drop the ravioli into the water. Do not crowd the pan.
Cook for 4-6 minutes. The ravioli are ready 1 minute after they all float to the surface.
Use a slotted spoon to gently lift the ravioli out. Never pour them into a colander to avoid tearing.

name: Tortellini
steps: In a medium pot, bring the broth to a boil (traditional 'En Brodo' style).
Add the tortellini. Stir gently.
If fresh, cook for 3-4 minutes. If dried, cook for 7-9 minutes.
The tortellini will absorb the flavor of the broth as they cook.
Serve hot in the broth with a sprinkle of parmesan cheese.

name: Seafood Pasta
steps: Scrub the clams and mussels under cold water. Discard any that are open and won't close.
Boil the pasta in salted water until very al dente.
In a large deep skillet, heat olive oil and sauté garlic until fragrant. Add cherry tomatoes and cook for 3 minutes.
Add all the seafood to the skillet. Pour in the white wine and cover with a lid.
Steam for 5 minutes. Remove the lid and discard any shellfish that haven't opened.
Transfer the pasta directly into the seafood pan. Toss over high heat for 2 minutes so the pasta absorbs the seafood liquor.
Top with fresh parsley and a drizzle of raw olive oil.

name: Vegetable Pasta
steps: Prepare the vegetables by cutting them into uniform bite-sized pieces.
Boil the pasta in salted water until al dente.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Sauté the peppers and zucchini for 4 minutes until they begin to char slightly.
Add the asparagus and garlic. Sauté for another 3 minutes until all vegetables are tender-crisp.
Add the cooked pasta to the skillet with a splash of pasta water.
Toss well and season with lemon zest, salt, and black pepper. The lemon zest is the key to brightening the vegetable flavors.

name: Chicken Pasta
steps: Boil the pasta in a large pot of salted water until al dente. Drain and reserve a splash of water.
In a large skillet, heat olive oil and sear the chicken slices until golden and cooked through. Season with salt and pepper, then remove from pan.
In the same skillet, sauté garlic for 1 minute. Pour in the cream and bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the parmesan until melted. Add the fresh spinach and stir until wilted.
Return the chicken and pasta to the skillet. Toss everything together until the sauce is creamy and coats the pasta perfectly.

name: Beef Pasta
steps: Cook the pasta in salted water until al dente.
In a heavy skillet, sear the beef strips over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onions and mushrooms in the beef fat until tender. Add the Worcestershire sauce and beef broth.
Simmer the liquid until reduced by half. Stir in the sour cream to create a rich sauce.
Return the beef and add the cooked pasta to the pan. Toss well to coat and serve hot.

name: Spicy Pasta
steps: Boil the penne until al dente.
In a skillet, brown the sausage meat, breaking it into small crumbles.
Add the garlic and chili paste (adjust to heat preference). Sauté for 2 minutes.
Pour in the crushed tomatoes and simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce is thick.
Toss the pasta into the spicy sauce with a splash of pasta water. Garnish with parsley.

name: Mushroom Pasta
steps: Cook tagliatelle in salted water.
In a large pan, melt half the butter. Sauté mushrooms over high heat without stirring too much to get a deep brown color.
Add the remaining butter, minced garlic, and thyme. Sauté until fragrant.
Reserve some pasta water. Add the cooked pasta and the water to the mushrooms.
Stir in the parmesan vigorously to create a creamy mushroom butter emulsion.

name: Ramen Bowl
steps: Heat the ramen broth in a small pot until steaming. Do not boil vigorously.
Cook the ramen noodles in a separate pot of unsalted water for 1.5 - 2 minutes. Drain well.
Place the noodles into a deep ramen bowl. Pour the hot broth over the noodles and straighten them with chopsticks.
Arrange the chashu pork, halved egg, bamboo shoots, and green onions neatly on top.
Slide the nori square into the side of the bowl so it stays crispy. Serve immediately.

name: Pho-style Noodle Soup

ingredients.name: Beef Bone Broth (Infused with Star Anise)

steps: Soak rice noodles in warm water for 20 minutes, then blanch in boiling water for 30 seconds. Drain and place in a large bowl.
Bring the aromatic beef broth to a rolling boil.
Lay the thinly sliced raw beef strips on top of the cooked noodles.
Pour the boiling broth directly over the beef—the heat from the broth will cook the thin meat instantly.
Top with bean sprouts, herbs, and serve with lime wedges and hoisin sauce on the side.

name: Udon Noodles
steps: Prepare udon noodles according to package instructions (frozen udon often only needs a 1-minute blanch).
Heat oil in a wok. Sauté onions and cabbage until softened and slightly charred.
Add the udon noodles to the wok.
Pour in the soy sauce and mirin. Toss over high heat for 2-3 minutes until the noodles absorb the sauce.
Plate and top with a handful of dancing bonito flakes.

name: Soba Noodles
steps: Boil soba noodles for 4-5 minutes in unsalted water. DO NOT overcook.
IMPORTANT: Drain and immediately plunge into an ice-water bath. Rub the noodles to remove excess starch.
Arrange the chilled noodles on a bamboo mat (Zaru).
Place the tsuyu sauce in a small side cup.
Garnish with nori and serve with wasabi and grated daikon to be mixed into the sauce by the diner.

name: Lo Mein
steps: Boil egg noodles until just tender. Rinse with cold water and toss with a drop of oil.
Sear the protein in a hot wok until cooked. Remove.
Stir-fry vegetables for 2 minutes. Add noodles and the pre-mixed sauce (Soy, Oyster, Sesame).
Toss everything together over very high heat for 2 minutes until steaming.
Serve immediately garnished with green onions.

name: Chow Mein
steps: Boil noodles for 2 minutes, drain, and let them dry on a tray.
Heat a large amount of oil in a wok. Fry the noodles undisturbed for 3 minutes per side until a 'pancake' forms and they are crispy.
Push noodles aside. Sauté garlic, cabbage, and celery.
Add dark soy sauce for color and flavor. Toss the crispy noodles with the vegetables.
The result should be a mix of soft and crunchy textures.

name: Pad Thai
steps: Soak noodles in room temperature water for 30 minutes until pliable. DO NOT boil.
Heat oil in a wok. Sauté shrimp until pink. Push to the side.
Crack the egg in the center, scramble it, then add the soaked noodles.
Pour in the pre-mixed sauce (Tamarind, Fish Sauce, Sugar). Toss vigorously.
Add bean sprouts and peanuts at the very end. Serve with a lime wedge.

name: Pad See Ew
steps: Marinate beef in a little oyster sauce and oil for 10 minutes.
Heat a wok until smoking hot. Sauté garlic and beef until seared.
Add the Chinese broccoli and noodles.
Pour in the black soy sauce and high-heat toss until the noodles are slightly caramelized and charred (Wok Hei).
The noodles should have dark brown patches and a smoky flavor.

name: Stir-fried Noodles
steps: Boil noodles and drain.
Sauté ginger and peppers in a hot pan.
Add noodles and soy sauce.
Stir-fry for 3 minutes until very hot and well coated.
Garnish with sesame seeds.

name: Rice Noodles
steps: Boil vermicelli for 2-3 minutes. Drain and rinse with cold water.
Place noodles in a bowl.
Arrange raw cucumber, pickled carrots, and mint leaves on top.
Pour the Nuoc Cham (lime, fish sauce, chili) over the bowl.
Serve cold as a refreshing noodle salad.

name: Glass Noodles
steps: Boil glass noodles for 7 minutes. Drain and cut them with scissors to make them easier to eat.
Blanch spinach, squeeze dry, and season with sesame oil.
Sauté mushrooms and onions separately.
Combine noodles, vegetables, soy sauce, and sesame oil in a large bowl.
Mix thoroughly by hand (using gloves) to ensure the noodles are glossy and savory. Sprinkle with sesame seeds.

name: Spicy Noodle Bowl
steps: In a small skillet, fry the ground pork with the preserved vegetables (Ya Cai) until the pork is crispy and dark brown. Set aside.
Prepare the sauce base in each serving bowl: whisk together chili oil, crushed Sichuan peppercorns, tahini, soy sauce, and a splash of black vinegar.
Bring the chicken broth to a simmer and whisk a portion into each bowl to create a creamy, spicy soup base.
Boil the noodles until just tender. Drain well but do not rinse.
Place the hot noodles into the bowls. Top with the crispy pork mixture, chopped scallions, and crushed peanuts.
Toss everything together before eating to ensure the numbing spice coats every strand.

name: Cold Noodle Dish
steps: Boil the thin noodles until fully cooked. Drain and immediately plunge into an ice-water bath.
Rub the noodles gently under cold water to remove excess starch, then drain thoroughly. Toss with 1 tsp of sesame oil to prevent sticking.
In a bowl, whisk peanut butter, vinegar, soy sauce, sugar, minced garlic, and the remaining sesame oil. Add a splash of warm water to reach a pourable consistency.
Place the chilled noodles in a shallow bowl and top with the julienned cucumber and bean sprouts.
Pour the peanut sauce over the top and garnish with toasted sesame seeds and fresh cilantro.

name: Beef Noodle Bowl
steps: Blanch the beef cubes in boiling water for 5 minutes to remove impurities. Drain and rinse with cold water.
In a heavy pot, sauté ginger, garlic, and star anise. Add the bean paste and fry until the oil turns red.
Add the beef, soy sauce, and rock sugar. Pour in enough water to cover the beef by 2 inches.
Simmer on very low heat for 2.5 hours until the beef is fork-tender. The broth should be dark and aromatic.
Boil the noodles and bok choy separately.
Assemble by placing noodles in a bowl, adding the beef chunks, and ladling the rich broth over the top. Garnish with pickled mustard greens.

name: Chicken Noodle Bowl
steps: Simmer the chicken thighs in water with coriander roots, garlic, and cinnamon for 30 minutes to create a light, clear broth.
Remove the chicken, shred the meat, and discard the bones. Strain the broth.
Soak the rice vermicelli in cold water for 10 minutes, then blanch in the boiling broth for 30 seconds.
Season the broth with fish sauce and a pinch of white sugar.
Place the noodles and shredded chicken in a bowl. Top with bean sprouts and a generous spoonful of crispy garlic oil.
Serve with a side of Thai chili powder and vinegar-soaked chilies for customization.

name: Vegetable Noodle Bowl
steps: Pan-fry the tofu cubes until golden on all sides. Set aside.
Sauté the mushrooms and carrots in a wok with a little ginger and garlic.
Cook the noodles according to the package and drain.
Whisk miso paste with a little warm water and toss with the warm noodles and vegetables.
Fold in the edamame and tofu gently.
Serve in a deep bowl garnished with toasted seaweed and a drizzle of sesame oil.

name: Seafood Noodle Bowl
steps: In a deep pot, fry the laksa paste in a little oil until fragrant and the oil separates.
Pour in the coconut milk and an equal amount of water. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add the fish balls and tofu puffs. Simmer for 5 minutes so the puffs absorb the spicy broth.
Add the shrimp and calamari. Cook for only 2-3 minutes until the seafood is just done.
Blanch the rice noodles in boiling water and place in a large bowl.
Ladle the rich, creamy seafood broth over the noodles and garnish with fresh Vietnamese mint and a squeeze of lime.

name: Spinach Pasta
steps: Boil the green spinach pasta in salted water until al dente.
In a large skillet, sauté minced garlic in butter. Add the heavy cream and bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the fresh ricotta until the sauce is creamy and thick. Season with salt and lots of white pepper.
Add the lemon zest and the cooked pasta to the skillet. Toss gently to avoid breaking the delicate noodles.
Serve topped with toasted pine nuts and a few fresh spinach leaves for color.

name: Light Pasta
steps: Cook the angel hair pasta for only 2-3 minutes. It cooks very fast.
In a large pan, heat olive oil and sauté the cherry tomatoes until they begin to burst.
Add the spiralized zucchini to the tomatoes for only 60 seconds (do not overcook or it becomes watery).
Toss the pasta into the pan with the vegetables. Add a splash of pasta water.
Finish with fresh basil, sea salt, and a crack of black pepper. This dish relies on the quality of the olive oil.

name: Protein Pasta
steps: Boil the high-protein pasta in salted water. Watch closely as pulse-based pasta can foam and overcook quickly.
Brown the ground turkey in a skillet with garlic powder and dried herbs.
Stir in the tomato passata and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in the Greek yogurt to create a high-protein 'rose' sauce.
Combine the pasta with the sauce. Top with hemp seeds for an extra protein and omega-3 boost.

name: Whole Wheat Pasta
steps: Boil the whole wheat spaghetti until tender but firm. This pasta usually takes 2-3 minutes longer than white pasta.
In a skillet, sauté the eggplant cubes in olive oil until golden and soft.
Add the roasted peppers and olives to the pan.
Toss the hot pasta with the vegetables and oil. The nutty flavor of the whole wheat pairs perfectly with roasted eggplant.
Finish with a generous amount of freshly cracked black pepper and a sprinkle of nutritional yeast (optional).

name: Gluten-Free Pasta
steps: Cook the gluten-free pasta in a large volume of water to prevent the starchy water from becoming too thick.
Rinse the pasta briefly with warm water after draining (unlike wheat pasta) to prevent it from becoming gummy.
In a mixing bowl, toss the warm pasta with the pesto sauce, steamed broccoli, and grilled chicken.
Add a splash of olive oil to help the sauce distribute evenly.
Serve warm. Gluten-free pasta is best eaten immediately as it hardens faster when cooling.

name: Curry Noodles
steps: Fry the red curry paste and turmeric in 1/2 cup of coconut milk until the oil separates.
Add the chicken and the rest of the coconut milk. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Divide the egg noodles into two portions. Boil one portion until soft.
Deep fry the second portion of noodles until crispy and golden. These act as the signature topping.
Serve the boiled noodles in a deep bowl with the curry broth and chicken. Top with the crispy noodles.
Garnish with sliced shallots, lime wedges, and pickled mustard greens.

name: Noodle Soup
steps: In a large pot, bring the chicken broth to a boil. Add the carrots and celery and simmer for 10 minutes until soft.
Add the egg noodles directly to the broth. Cook for 6-8 minutes until tender.
Stir in the pre-cooked shredded chicken to heat through.
Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh parsley.
Taste and adjust salt and pepper. Serve hot in large deep bowls for ultimate comfort.

name: Wok Noodles
steps: Blanch thick noodles for 2 minutes and drain.
Heat a wok until it smokes. Add oil and sear the pork strips until browned.
Add the cabbage and stir-fry for 2 minutes over high heat.
Add the noodles and both soy sauces. Toss rapidly using a metal spatula to ensure the noodles get a deep brown color.
Add the garlic chives at the very last second. Toss for 30 seconds more and serve hot with a side of chili paste.

name: Street-style Noodles
steps: Heat a wok over extremely high heat. Fry the Chinese sausage and shrimp until aromatic.
Push to the side and crack an egg into the center. Scramble lightly.
Add the flat rice noodles and the bean sprouts.
Pour in the dark soy sauce and a spoonful of sambal chili paste.
Toss everything together for only 1-2 minutes. The goal is 'Char' (slight burnt edges) without overcooking the sprouts.
Serve on a banana leaf for an authentic street-food experience.

name: Korean Noodle Bowl (Japchae)
steps: Boil the glass noodles for 7 minutes. Drain and rinse under cold water. Use kitchen scissors to cut them into manageable lengths.
Blanch the spinach for 30 seconds, squeeze out all excess water, and season with a drop of sesame oil and salt.
Stir-fry the beef, carrots, and mushrooms separately in a wok to maintain their individual colors and textures.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the noodles, cooked vegetables, and beef.
Pour in the soy sauce, sesame oil, and a teaspoon of sugar. Mix thoroughly by hand (use a glove) to ensure the noodles are perfectly glossy.
Garnish with sesame seeds and serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Japanese Noodle Bowl (Hiyashi Chuka)
steps: Whisk soy sauce, rice vinegar, sesame oil, sugar, and a bit of water to create the cold dressing. Chill in the fridge.
Cook ramen noodles until tender. Rinse thoroughly in an ice-water bath and drain until dry.
Make a very thin omelet, roll it up, and slice into fine threads (Kinshi Tamago).
Place the chilled noodles in a shallow bowl.
Arrange the ham, cucumber, and egg threads in colorful piles on top of the noodles.
Pour the cold dressing over the bowl and garnish with pickled ginger and a dab of hot mustard.

name: Cold Pasta Salad
steps: Boil the pasta in salted water until fully cooked (avoid al dente here as cold pasta firms up).
Drain and rinse with cold water to stop the cooking process immediately.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled pasta with tomatoes, mozzarella, olives, and salami.
Pour the Italian dressing over the mixture and toss until everything is evenly coated.
Let the salad sit in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to allow the pasta to absorb the flavors.
Toss again and garnish with fresh parsley before serving.

name: Mediterranean Pasta
steps: Cook linguine in boiling salted water until al dente.
While pasta cooks, chop the artichokes and sun-dried tomatoes into bite-sized pieces.
In a large bowl, whisk together the lemon juice and olive oil with a pinch of dried oregano.
Reserve 2 tablespoons of pasta water and then drain the pasta.
Toss the hot pasta into the bowl with the dressing and reserved water.
Add the artichokes, tomatoes, and olives. Mix well and top with crumbled feta cheese.

name: Italian Classic Pasta (Cacio e Pepe)
steps: Toast the whole peppercorns in a dry pan until fragrant, then crush them coarsely in a mortar and pestle.
Boil the pasta in a small amount of water (use less water than usual to make it extra starchy).
In a bowl, mix the finely grated Pecorino with a little warm pasta water to create a thick paste.
Transfer the al dente pasta to the pan with the toasted pepper. Add a ladle of pasta water and toss.
Remove the pan from the heat (CRITICAL: if too hot, the cheese will clump).
Stir in the cheese paste vigorously until it melts into a creamy, emulsified sauce that coats the noodles.

name: Asian Noodle Bowl
steps: Cook the noodles, drain, and set aside.
Heat oil in a wok. Fry the tofu cubes until golden on all sides.
Add the ginger and garlic, followed by the snap peas. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the noodles to the wok.
Pour in the soy sauce and a splash of water. Toss everything over high heat until the noodles are well-seasoned and steaming.
Serve in a bowl with a drizzle of chili oil if desired.

name: Chinese Noodle Bowl (Dan Dan Noodles)
steps: In a small bowl, whisk together sesame paste, chili oil, soy sauce, and sugar to make the sauce.
Sauté the ground pork and preserved mustard (Ya Cai) in a skillet until the pork is crispy and browned.
Boil the noodles until tender. Drain.
Place the sesame-chili sauce at the bottom of a deep bowl.
Add the noodles on top, followed by the crispy pork mixture.
Garnish with crushed peanuts and chopped scallions. Mix the sauce from the bottom before eating.

name: Thai Noodle Bowl (Boat Noodles Style)
steps: Prepare a deep broth by simmering beef stock with star anise, cinnamon, and dark soy sauce for 20 minutes.
Soak the thin rice noodles in warm water for 10 minutes, then blanch in boiling water for 30 seconds.
Place the noodles in a bowl.
Lay the raw beef slices over the noodles.
Pour the boiling hot broth over the beef so it cooks instantly.
Garnish with fresh Thai basil and crispy pork rinds for texture.

name: Vietnamese Noodle Bowl (Bun Bo Nam Bo)
steps: Boil the vermicelli for 2 minutes, rinse with cold water, and place in a bowl.
Marinate the beef with minced lemongrass and garlic for 15 minutes.
Stir-fry the beef over high heat for only 2-3 minutes until charred but tender.
Arrange raw lettuce and herbs at the bottom of the noodle bowl.
Place the beef on top of the vermicelli.
Top with crushed peanuts and fried shallots. Pour the Nuoc Cham (lime-fish sauce) over everything and toss.

name: White Rice Bowl
steps: IMPORTANT: Rinse the rice under cold water at least 3 times until the water runs clear to remove surface starch.
Place rice, water, and salt in a medium pot.
Bring to a boil, then immediately reduce heat to the lowest setting.
Cover with a tight-fitting lid and simmer for 15-18 minutes without opening the lid.
Turn off heat and let the rice steam undisturbed for 5 minutes.
Add the butter and fluff gently with a fork to separate the grains.

name: Brown Rice Bowl
steps: Rinse the brown rice. In a pot, combine rice and water and bring to a boil.
Cover and simmer on low for 40-45 minutes. Brown rice takes significantly longer than white rice to soften.
Let the rice sit covered for 10 minutes after cooking to finish steaming.
Place a base of warm brown rice in a bowl.
Top with massaged kale (kale rubbed with oil/salt) and avocado slices.
Drizzle with tahini dressing and serve as a nutrient-dense bowl.

name: Egg Fried Rice
steps: Use your hands to break up any large clumps of the cold, day-old rice.
Heat a wok over high heat with 2 tablespoons of oil. Pour in the beaten eggs.
Scramble the eggs until they are 80% cooked, then push them to the side.
Add the cold rice to the wok. Stir-fry vigorously, tossing the rice with the egg.
Pour the soy sauce around the edges of the wok so it sizzles before mixing with the rice.
Toss in the green onions and sesame oil. Fry for 1 more minute until the rice is steaming and slightly toasted.

name: Chicken Fried Rice
steps: Sear the diced chicken in a hot wok with oil until golden and cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same wok, stir-fry the garlic and frozen vegetables for 2 minutes.
Add the cold rice. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes, pressing down on the rice to ensure even heating.
Return the chicken to the wok.
Add the soy sauce and a pinch of white pepper. Toss everything together over high heat until the rice is 'dancing' in the pan.
Serve hot immediately.

name: Vegetable Fried Rice
steps: Heat oil in a wok. Stir-fry the carrots and corn for 2 minutes.
Add the bok choy stems first, then the leaves, sautéing until wilted.
Add the cold rice and use the spatula to break it apart.
Fry for 5 minutes, ensuring the vegetables are well distributed throughout the rice.
Season with soy sauce and salt to taste. A dash of toasted sesame oil at the end adds great aroma.

name: Shrimp Fried Rice
steps: Stir-fry the shrimp in a very hot wok with oil for only 2 minutes until they turn pink. Remove immediately to avoid overcooking.
Sauté the onions and garlic in the same wok until aromatic.
Add the cold rice and stir-fry for 4 minutes until the rice is hot and toasted.
Add the shrimp back to the wok along with the soy sauce.
Toss for 1 minute so the flavors meld and the shrimp are heated through.
Garnish with sliced green onions and serve.

name: Fried Plantain Rice Plate
steps: Wash the rice thoroughly and cook it with the black-eyed peas in lightly salted water until tender.
Peel the plantains and slice them diagonally into 1cm thick ovals.
Heat oil in a skillet and fry the plantain slices until golden brown and caramelized on both sides. Set aside on paper towels.
In a separate pan, sauté sliced onions and tomato paste to create a light stew base.
Toss the cooked rice and beans into the onion-tomato mixture.
Serve the rice and beans on a plate with a generous portion of fried plantains on the side.

name: Thieboudienne-style Plate
steps: Stuff the fish with a parsley-garlic-chili paste (Rof). Sear the fish in hot oil in a heavy pot, then remove.
In the same oil, fry onions and tomato paste until a deep red base forms. Add water and bring to a boil.
Add the cassava, carrots, eggplant, and whole habañero. Simmer until vegetables are tender.
Remove the vegetables and set aside with the fish. Add the broken rice to the remaining boiling stock.
The liquid level should be exactly 1 inch above the rice. Cover tightly and steam on very low heat for 25 minutes.
Serve the red rice at the bottom of a large platter, topped with the fish and boiled vegetables.

name: West African Rice Plate
steps: Blend peppers, tomatoes, and scotch bonnet into a smooth purée and boil down until the water evaporates.
In a large pot, fry onions in oil, then add the concentrated pepper paste and fry for 10 minutes (the secret to Jollof flavor).
Stir in ginger, garlic, thyme, and the bouillon cube.
Add the rice and just enough chicken stock to cover. Cover the pot with foil and a lid to trap steam.
Cook on low heat for 30 minutes without opening the lid. The rice should absorb all the red sauce.
Fluff with a fork and serve with grilled chicken or fish.

name: Spanish Rice (Arroz Rojo)
steps: Heat olive oil in a skillet. Add the dry rice and sauté until it turns golden brown and smells nutty.
Add minced garlic and sauté for 30 seconds. Do not burn the garlic.
Pour in the tomato sauce, chicken broth, and cumin. Stir once to combine.
Bring to a boil, then cover and reduce heat to low.
Simmer for 20 minutes until the liquid is fully absorbed. Let it rest for 5 minutes before fluffing with a fork.

name: Mexican Rice Bowl
steps: Warm the cooked rice and black beans in a microwave or small pot.
In a wide bowl, place the warm rice as the base.
Arrange the black beans, corn, and shredded lettuce in separate sections around the rice.
Top with a generous scoop of fresh salsa and the sliced avocado.
Garnish with fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lime before serving. This is an ideal healthy, quick meal.

name: Cilantro Lime Rice
steps: Rinse the basmati rice until the water runs clear to ensure fluffy, non-sticky grains.
Cook the rice with water and a pinch of salt until tender (about 15-18 mins).
Once cooked, stir in the butter and let it melt into the hot rice.
Fold in the chopped cilantro, lime juice, and lime zest gently with a fork.
The result should be fragrant, bright, and the perfect side for tacos or grilled protein.

name: Turmeric Rice
steps: Sauté the minced onion in a little oil until translucent.
Add the turmeric powder and the dry rice. Stir for 1 minute until the grains are coated in yellow oil.
Pour in the chicken broth and add the bay leaf.
Cover and simmer on low for 15-17 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the rice steam for 5 minutes before removing the bay leaf and fluffing.

name: Coconut Rice
steps: Rinse the jasmine rice thoroughly.
In a pot, combine the coconut milk, water, sugar, and salt. Stir to dissolve the sugar.
Add the rice to the coconut liquid and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to the lowest setting, cover tightly, and simmer for 15-18 minutes.
The rice will be rich and slightly sweet. Serve as a base for curries or with mango as a dessert.

name: Saffron Rice
steps: Crush the saffron threads in a mortar and pestle and soak them in 2 tablespoons of hot water for 10 minutes to release the color and aroma.
Melt butter in a pot. Sauté the dry rice for 1 minute.
Add the chicken stock and bring to a boil. Simmer on low, covered, for 15 minutes.
Once the rice is cooked, drizzle the concentrated saffron water over 1/3 of the rice in the pot.
Gently fold the yellow saffron rice into the white rice to create a beautiful two-tone effect.

name: Risotto (Classic Milanese)
steps: In a wide saucepan, sauté shallots in butter until translucent.
Add the rice and toast for 2 minutes until the edges are translucent.
Pour in the white wine and stir constantly until fully absorbed.
Begin adding hot broth one ladle at a time, stirring frequently. Wait for the rice to absorb the liquid before adding more.
After 15 minutes, add the saffron. Continue the broth process for 18-20 minutes total.
Remove from heat and stir in a knob of butter and the parmesan (Mantecatura) to achieve a creamy consistency.

name: Mushroom Risotto
steps: Sauté the mushrooms with garlic and thyme in butter until golden and all liquid has evaporated. Remove half for garnish.
Proceed with the risotto base (shallots, rice, wine) as in the classic method.
Add the chopped soaked porcini mushrooms and their strained soaking liquid to the rice early on for a deep earthy flavor.
Continue adding hot stock ladle by ladle, stirring frequently.
Fold in the cooked mushrooms and parmesan at the end.
Top with the reserved crispy mushrooms before serving.

name: Seafood Risotto
steps: Quickly sear the shrimp and scallops in a pan with garlic and olive oil. Remove when just cooked.
Make the risotto using seafood stock. Do not add cheese at the end (traditional Italian rule for seafood).
When the rice is al dente, fold in the cooked seafood and any juices from the pan.
Stir in the fresh parsley and lemon zest for brightness.
Add a splash of high-quality olive oil instead of butter for the final texture.

name: Vegetable Risotto
steps: Blanch the asparagus and peas for 2 minutes in the stock, then remove and set aside.
Start the risotto base. Add the zucchini cubes midway through the cooking process.
Stir in the chopped spinach when the rice is almost done so it just wilts.
Fold in the blanched asparagus and peas at the very end to keep them bright green.
Finish with parmesan and a touch of lemon juice.

name: Quinoa Bowl
steps: Rinse quinoa under cold water. Boil with 2 cups of water for 15 minutes until the 'tails' emerge.
Drain any excess water and fluff the quinoa. Let it cool slightly.
In a large bowl, toss the quinoa with diced cucumbers, tomatoes, and chickpeas.
Add the lemon vinaigrette and mix well.
Top with crumbled feta and fresh herbs like mint or parsley.

name: Roast Chicken
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Pat the chicken completely dry with paper towels (essential for crispy skin).
Stuff the cavity with the lemon halves, garlic head, and herb sprigs.
Rub the softened butter all over the skin, especially the breast and legs. Season generously with salt and pepper.
Place the chicken on a roasting rack in a pan. Roast for 1 hour to 1 hour 15 minutes.
Check the temperature in the thickest part of the thigh; it should reach 75°C (165°F).
IMPORTANT: Let the chicken rest for 15 minutes before carving to allow the juices to redistribute.

name: Lemon Herb Chicken
steps: In a small bowl, whisk olive oil, lemon juice, zest, minced garlic, and chopped herbs to create a marinade.
Place chicken breasts between plastic wrap and pound to an even 1-inch thickness for uniform cooking.
Marinate the chicken for at least 20 minutes in the refrigerator.
Heat a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chicken for 6-7 minutes on the first side without moving it to develop a golden crust.
Flip the chicken, add a knob of butter to the pan, and baste the chicken with the melting butter and lemon juices for another 5-6 minutes.
Ensure the internal temperature reaches 75°C (165°F). Let rest for 5 minutes before slicing to keep it juicy.

name: Couscous Salad
steps: Bring 1 cup of salted water or vegetable broth to a boil. Pour over the dry couscous in a heat-proof bowl.
Cover tightly with plastic wrap and let sit for 5 minutes. Do not stir.
Remove the cover and fluff the couscous thoroughly with a fork to break up any clumps.
Once cooled to room temperature, add the diced cucumber, halved tomatoes, and chickpeas.
Fold in the fresh herbs and pour the lemon vinaigrette over the salad.
Toss gently and let sit for 10 minutes before serving to allow the grains to absorb the dressing.

name: Bulgur Wheat Salad (Tabbouleh Style)
steps: Rinse the bulgur wheat and soak it in the lemon juice and olive oil for 20 minutes until softened (traditional 'no-cook' soaking).
IMPORTANT: Wash and dry the parsley completely. Chop it very finely with a sharp knife by hand; do not use a food processor as it will bruise the leaves.
Strain any excess juice from the diced tomatoes to prevent the salad from becoming soggy.
In a large bowl, combine the chopped herbs, green onions, and tomatoes.
Mix in the soaked bulgur wheat and any remaining liquid from the soak.
Season with salt and a touch of allspice. Serve with romaine lettuce leaves used as scoops.

name: Farro Bowl
steps: Boil farro in salted water (like pasta) for 20-25 minutes until chewy and tender. Drain well.
While farro cooks, roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C with olive oil and salt until tender.
Massage the kale with a drop of olive oil and salt for 1 minute until it turns dark green and softens.
Whisk tahini, lemon juice, maple syrup, and a splash of warm water to make a creamy dressing.
Assemble the bowl by layering farro at the bottom, followed by kale, sweet potatoes, and pumpkin seeds.
Drizzle generously with the tahini dressing and serve warm.

name: Barley Dish (Mushroom Pearled Barley)
steps: In a pot, sauté shallots and garlic in oil until soft. Add mushrooms and cook until browned.
Stir in the pearled barley and toast for 2 minutes to bring out a nutty flavor.
Add the vegetable broth and thyme. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 30-35 minutes until the barley is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Season with salt and plenty of black pepper. Fold in a small knob of butter for extra richness before serving.

name: Wild Rice Salad
steps: Cook the wild rice blend in 2 cups of water for 40-45 minutes. Drain any excess liquid and fluff.
While the rice is warm, toss it with the cranberries so they plump up slightly.
Once cooled, add the diced apple (toss apple in a little lemon juice first to prevent browning).
Add the toasted pecans and scallions.
Whisk olive oil and apple cider vinegar together, then pour over the rice.
Season with salt and serve at room temperature. This salad develops better flavor if chilled for 1 hour.

name: Sushi Bowl (Deconstructed Sushi)
steps: Prepare sushi rice and season with rice vinegar, sugar, and salt. Let it cool to room temperature.
Place a base of seasoned rice in a bowl.
Arrange the salmon cubes, edamame, and cucumber slices on top of the rice in separate sections.
Add a dollop of pickled ginger and a small mound of wasabi to the side.
Drizzle with spicy mayo and sprinkle with nori strips and sesame seeds.
Serve with a side of low-sodium soy sauce.

name: Poke Bowl
steps: In a small bowl, marinate the tuna cubes with soy sauce, sesame oil, and a pinch of chili flakes for 15 minutes.
Place warm (not hot) rice at the bottom of the bowl.
Top with the marinated tuna and the seaweed salad.
Add fresh toppings: sliced avocado, radishes, and shredded carrots.
Sprinkle with furikake seasoning or toasted sesame seeds.
Serve cold with extra soy sauce on the side.

name: Bibimbap-style Bowl
steps: Sauté mushrooms and carrots separately with a drop of sesame oil. Blanch and season the greens.
Stir-fry the ground beef with a little soy sauce, garlic, and sugar until browned.
Place warm rice in a deep bowl.
Arrange the colorful vegetables and the beef in distinct sections around the perimeter of the bowl.
Place the sunny-side-up egg in the center.
Add a generous dollop of Gochujang sauce. To eat, mix everything together thoroughly until the rice is red and well-coated.

name: Rice and Beans Plate
steps: Rinse rice until water runs clear. In a pot, combine rice, kidney beans (with half their liquid), coconut milk, and enough water to cover by 1 inch.
Add the smashed garlic, whole thyme sprigs, and the whole scotch bonnet pepper.
CRITICAL: Do not burst the pepper, or the rice will be too hot to eat. It is for aroma only.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to the lowest setting and cover with a tight lid.
Simmer for 18-20 minutes until the rice is tender and the liquid is absorbed.
Remove the thyme and the whole pepper. Fluff with a fork and serve with grilled chicken or fish.

name: Mediterranean Rice Plate
steps: Cook the basmati rice with a pinch of turmeric for a golden color. Fluff and place on a large plate.
Arrange the pre-cooked chicken souvlaki skewers or pieces on top of the rice.
Place a scoop of creamy hummus and a mound of fresh cucumber-tomato salad on the side.
Sprinkle crumbled feta cheese and dried oregano over the entire plate.
Drizzle with high-quality olive oil and a squeeze of fresh lemon.
Serve with a warm pita bread on the side for a complete Mediterranean experience.

name: Middle Eastern Rice (Mujadara)
steps: Boil lentils in 2 cups of water for 15 minutes (they should be 70% cooked). Drain.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet. Fry the sliced onions on medium heat for 20 minutes until they are deep mahogany brown and caramelized (not burnt). Remove half for garnish.
In a pot, combine the rice, lentils, the remaining half of the caramelized onions, and cumin.
Add 1.5 cups of water and salt. Bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 15-18 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let steam for 5 minutes.
Serve topped with the reserved crispy caramelized onions and a dollop of cold Greek yogurt.

name: Asian Rice Plate (Hainanese Chicken Style)
steps: Simmer the chicken in water with ginger and garlic for 30 minutes until tender. Remove chicken and immediately submerge in ice water to keep the skin elastic. Save the broth.
In a pot, sauté minced garlic and ginger in oil. Add the dry rice and toast for 1 minute.
Pour the chicken broth over the rice (use a 1:1.5 ratio) and add a pandan leaf if available.
Cook the rice normally. The rice will absorb the fat and aroma of the chicken stock.
Slice the cold chicken into pieces. Serve on a plate next to the fragrant rice.
Provide a side of ginger-scallion oil and dark soy sauce for dipping.

name: Curry Rice Plate (Japanese Beef Curry)
steps: In a heavy pot, sauté the onions until they are very soft and brown (about 10 mins).
Add the beef cubes and brown them on all sides.
Add the carrots and potatoes, then pour in 3 cups of water. Simmer for 25 minutes until the beef is tender.
Turn off the heat. Break the curry roux block into pieces and stir into the liquid until fully dissolved.
Turn the heat back to low and simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce is thick and glossy.
Serve the hot curry on half of a large plate, with a mound of steamed white rice on the other half. Garnish with red pickles (Fukujinzuke).

name: Sticky Rice Bowl (Mango Sticky Rice)
steps: Soak sticky rice in water for at least 4 hours (overnight is best). Drain.
Steam the rice in a steamer basket lined with cheesecloth for 25-30 minutes until tender.
While the rice is hot, whisk warm coconut cream with sugar and a pinch of salt.
Pour 3/4 of the coconut mixture over the warm rice and let it sit for 20 minutes to absorb the liquid.
Serve the sweet rice in a bowl, topped with fresh mango slices.
Drizzle the remaining coconut cream on top and sprinkle with toasted mung beans for crunch.

name: Jasmin Rice Bowl
steps: Rinse the jasmine rice in a bowl of cold water. Swirl gently and drain; repeat 3 times until the water is clear to ensure the grains don't clump.
Place the rice and water in a medium saucepan. Add a pinch of salt and a tied pandan leaf for authentic fragrance.
Bring to a rolling boil over high heat, then immediately reduce heat to the lowest setting.
Cover with a tight-fitting lid and simmer for 15 minutes without lifting the lid.
Remove from heat and let the rice steam undisturbed for an additional 10 minutes.
Discard the pandan leaf and fluff with a rice paddle or fork. Serve as a base for stir-fries or curries.

name: Basmati Rice Bowl
steps: Soak the basmati rice in cold water for 30 minutes. This allows the long grains to expand without breaking during cooking.
In a pot, melt ghee over medium heat. Add the cardamom pods and cumin seeds; fry for 1 minute until they sizzle and release aroma.
Drain the soaked rice and add it to the pot. Stir gently for 1 minute to coat every grain in the aromatic fat.
Add water and salt. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover tightly.
Cook for 12-15 minutes until the water is absorbed. Turn off the heat and let sit for 5 minutes.
Fluff gently with a fork to maintain the length of the grains. Serve hot.

name: Healthy Rice Bowl
steps: Cook the wild/black rice in salted water (ratio 1:2) for 20-30 minutes until tender but chewy. Drain any excess water.
While the rice is hot, stir in the grated fresh turmeric to infuse the color and antioxidants.
Place the rice at the bottom of a large bowl.
Arrange the steamed edamame, raw purple cabbage, and shredded carrots in separate piles on top.
Sprinkle with pumpkin seeds for crunch and healthy fats.
Drizzle the lemon tahini dressing over the bowl just before eating.

name: Protein Rice Bowl
steps: Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the ground meat with garlic powder until fully cooked.
Push the meat to one side and pour the egg whites into the other side. Scramble until set, then mix with the meat.
Add the pre-cooked warm rice to the skillet.
Pour in the liquid aminos and toss everything together for 2 minutes to ensure even distribution of protein.
Serve in a bowl. This dish is designed for maximum protein absorption and easy digestion.

name: Grain and Veggie Bowl
steps: Boil the farro in plenty of salted water for 20 minutes until tender but firm. Drain well.
Toss zucchini slices in olive oil and grill on a pan until char marks appear.
Place the farro in a bowl and season with a squeeze of lemon and a drop of oil.
Top with the grilled zucchini, roasted peppers, and olives.
Add a large dollop of hummus in the center as a creamy element.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve at room temperature.

name: Pilaf (Classic Rice Pilau)
steps: In a saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Sauté the onions until translucent but not browned.
Add the dry rice to the onions and butter. 'Toast' the rice for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly until the grains are coated and smell nutty.
Pour in the boiling broth and add the bay leaf.
Cover the pot and simmer on low for 15-18 minutes.
Let the pilaf rest covered for 5 minutes. Remove the bay leaf.
Fluff with a fork—the grains should be separate and aromatic, never mushy.

name: Dirty Rice

ingredients.name: The Holy Trinity (Onion, Celery, Bell Pepper)

steps: In a large skillet, brown the ground pork and finely minced chicken livers. The liver gives the rice its traditional 'dirty' color and deep savory flavor.
Add the onion, celery, and bell pepper (The Holy Trinity). Sauté for 5 minutes until soft.
Stir in the Cajun seasoning and garlic.
Deglaze the pan with beef broth, scraping up all the brown bits from the bottom.
Add the cooked white rice to the skillet. Toss thoroughly over medium heat for 5 minutes until the rice has absorbed the juices and spices.
Garnish with plenty of chopped scallions and serve hot.

name: Seafood Platter
steps: Pat all seafood completely dry—moisture is the enemy of a good sear.
Heat a large flat griddle or wide skillet with a little olive oil until smoking hot.
Place the fish fillet skin-side down and sear for 4 minutes. Flip and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the shrimp and calamari to the empty spaces of the pan. Cook for 2 minutes total.
Steam the mussels separately in a small pot with a splash of white wine for 3 minutes until they open.
Melt garlic butter in a small pan. Arrange all the seafood on a large warm platter.
Pour the hot garlic butter over everything and garnish with lemon wedges and parsley.

name: Shrimp Plate
steps: Toss the shrimp with Old Bay seasoning, salt, and minced garlic.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Sear shrimp for 90 seconds per side until they form a 'C' shape.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice directly into the pan at the last second to deglaze.
Arrange the shrimp on a plate next to a serving of creamy coleslaw and a wedge of lemon.
Serve with cocktail sauce if desired.

name: Garlic Butter Chicken
steps: Season chicken medallions with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp of oil in a skillet. Sear chicken for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through. Remove chicken.
Wipe the pan and add the butter. Melt over medium heat until it foams.
Add minced garlic and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Do not brown.
Add the chicken broth and whisk vigorously to create a light emulsion with the butter.
Return the chicken to the pan and toss to coat in the glossy garlic sauce. Garnish with parsley.

name: Spicy Chicken Plate
steps: Whisk honey, lime juice, and spices to make a glaze.
Sear chicken thighs skin-side down in a hot pan for 8 minutes to render the fat.
Flip the chicken and brush the spicy glaze onto the crispy skin.
Transfer the pan to a 200°C oven for 10 minutes to finish cooking and caramelize the glaze.
Serve with grilled corn and extra lime wedges.

name: BBQ Chicken
steps: Rub chicken with salt, pepper, and smoked paprika.
Grill over medium indirect heat (not directly over the flame) for 30 minutes, turning once.
Thin the BBQ sauce with apple cider vinegar.
During the last 10 minutes of cooking, baste the chicken generously with the sauce every 3 minutes.
Cooking the sauce at the end prevents the sugars from burning while creating a sticky, lacquered finish.

name: Grilled Steak
steps: Take the steak out of the fridge 30 minutes before cooking to reach room temperature (essential for an even sear).
Pat the meat extremely dry and season heavily with salt and pepper.
Heat a cast-iron skillet until it is smoking hot. Add 1 tbsp of high-smoke point oil.
Sear the steak for 3 minutes without moving it. Flip.
Add a knob of butter, the garlic clove, and the rosemary to the pan. Baste the steak with the foaming butter for 2 more minutes (for medium-rare).
IMPORTANT: Let the steak rest on a board for 8 minutes before slicing to retain the juices.

name: Steak Frites
steps: Fries: Soak potato batons in cold water for 30 mins. Pat dry. Double-fry: first at 150°C for 5 mins (to soften), then at 190°C for 2 mins (to crisp).
Steak: Sear the bavette over very high heat for 3 minutes per side. Slice against the grain.
Sauce: Sauté minced shallots in the steak pan with a little butter and vinegar.
Serve the sliced steak alongside a tall pile of salted hot fries.
Traditional bistro style uses no heavy gravy, just the natural juices of the meat.

name: Garlic Butter Steak
steps: Season the steak with salt and pepper.
Sear in a hot pan for 3 minutes per side to develop a deep brown crust.
In the final 3 minutes of cooking, lower the heat slightly and add the butter, garlic, and thyme.
Tilt the pan and use a spoon to continuously scoop the hot garlic butter over the steak.
Remove steak and let it rest. Pour the remaining garlic butter from the pan over the meat right before serving.

name: Pepper Steak
steps: Toss the sliced beef with cornstarch and a tablespoon of soy sauce. Let it marinate for 10 minutes to 'velvet' the meat.
Heat a wok or large skillet over very high heat with 1 tablespoon of oil.
Sear the beef in a single layer until browned and slightly crispy on the edges. Remove and set aside.
Add the peppers and ginger to the pan. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until the peppers are vibrant but still have a crunch.
Return the beef to the pan. Pour in the beef broth, remaining soy sauce, and the heavy amount of black pepper.
Toss continuously for 1 minute as the sauce thickens into a glossy glaze. Serve immediately over white rice.

name: Roast Beef
steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C (425°F). Use a small knife to make deep slits in the beef and insert garlic slivers.
Rub the entire roast with Dijon mustard, olive oil, chopped rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Place the roast on a rack in a roasting pan. Cook at 220°C for 15 minutes to sear the exterior.
Lower the oven temperature to 160°C (325°F) and continue roasting for about 20 minutes per 500g for medium-rare.
Remove the beef when the internal temperature reaches 52°C (125°F).
CRITICAL: Tent with foil and let the roast rest for at least 20 minutes before carving. This ensures the juices stay inside the meat.

name: Beef Medallions
steps: Season the medallions heavily with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a heavy skillet over high heat. Sear the medallions for 3-4 minutes per side for medium-rare.
Remove the beef and let it rest on a warm plate.
In the same pan, sauté shallots in the beef fat. Deglaze with red wine, scraping up the browned bits (fond).
Add the beef stock and simmer until reduced by half. Stir in a cold knob of butter to create a glossy pan sauce.
Pour the sauce over the medallions and serve immediately.

name: Grilled Pork Chop
steps: Pat the pork chops dry. Rub with olive oil and a blend of sage, thyme, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Grill the chops for 5-6 minutes per side. Do not overcook; pork is best when slightly blushing in the center.
Use tongs to hold the chops upright on their fatty edge for 1 minute to render and crisp the fat cap.
Internal temperature should be 63°C (145°F). Let rest for 5 minutes under foil.
Finish with a squeeze of fresh lemon juice to cut through the richness.

name: Glazed Pork
steps: Whisk honey, soy sauce, hoisin, and garlic in a small bowl to create the glaze.
Sear the pork tenderloin in a hot oven-safe skillet until browned on all sides.
Generously brush the glaze over the pork.
Transfer the skillet to a 200°C (400°F) oven. Roast for 12-15 minutes, basting with more glaze every 5 minutes.
The glaze should become sticky and dark. Remove once the internal temp is 63°C.
Slice into medallions and pour any remaining pan glaze over the top.

name: Roast Pork
steps: Score the pork fat in a diamond pattern. Rub with salt and crushed fennel seeds.
Place onions and carrots at the bottom of a roasting pan to act as a natural rack.
Place the pork on the vegetables and add the chicken broth to the bottom of the pan.
Roast at 180°C (350°F) until the internal temperature reaches 68°C. This may take 1.5 to 2 hours.
If the top isn't crispy, turn on the broiler for the last 5 minutes.
Let the roast rest for 15 minutes. Strain the pan juices and use as a light gravy.

name: Lamb Chops
steps: Mix garlic, mint, olive oil, and lemon juice. Rub into the lamb chops and marinate for 20 minutes.
Heat a skillet over high heat until it smokes slightly.
Sear the chops for 3 minutes on the first side to develop a crust. Flip.
Cook for another 2-3 minutes for medium-rare.
Hold the chops with tongs on their fatty edges to crisp up the fat for 1 minute.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving. The meat should be juicy and pink inside.

name: Roast Lamb
steps: Make deep slits in the lamb and insert garlic halves. Rub generously with lemon juice, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Place the lamb in a large roasting pan. Arrange the potatoes around the meat.
Drizzle everything with olive oil and add a half cup of water to the pan.
Cover tightly with foil and roast at 170°C (325°F) for 1.5 hours.
Remove the foil, increase the heat to 200°C, and roast for another 30 minutes to brown the lamb and crisp the potatoes.
Let rest for 20 minutes before carving.

name: Grilled Salmon
steps: Pat the salmon skin completely dry. Rub the flesh side with olive oil, dill, salt, and pepper.
Clean and oil the grill grates thoroughly to prevent sticking.
Place the salmon on the hot grill, skin-side down.
Grill undisturbed for 6 minutes. The skin should become crispy and release easily from the grate.
Carefully flip and grill for another 2-3 minutes. The center should be slightly translucent.
Serve with charred lemon slices.

name: Baked Fish
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Lightly grease a baking dish.
Mix breadcrumbs, parmesan, lemon zest, and melted butter in a small bowl.
Place the fish fillet in the dish and season with salt.
Press the breadcrumb mixture firmly onto the top of the fish.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the topping is golden and the fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Serve immediately with a side of steamed greens.

name: Lemon Herb Fish
steps: Whisk oil, lemon juice, garlic, and herbs to create a marinade.
Place the fish fillet on a large piece of parchment paper or aluminum foil.
Pour the herb mixture over the fish.
Fold the paper/foil into a sealed packet (En Papillote).
Bake at 190°C for 12 minutes. The steam inside the packet cooks the fish gently and keeps it moist.
Open carefully (watch for hot steam) and serve directly from the paper.

name: Scallops Plate
steps: IMPORTANT: Remove the small side muscle from each scallop. Pat the scallops extremely dry with paper towels.
Heat a skillet over high heat with 1 tsp of oil until smoking hot.
Place scallops in the pan. Do not crowd them. Sear for 2 minutes without moving them to get a golden crust.
Flip the scallops. Add the butter and garlic to the pan.
Baste the scallops with the melting butter for only 1 minute.
Serve immediately while the center is still tender and buttery.
equipment: Stainless Steel or Cast Iron Skillet

name: Grilled Shrimp Skewers
steps: Soak wooden skewers in water for 20 minutes to prevent them from burning on the grill.
Toss the shrimp with oil, garlic, lemon zest, and chili flakes.
Thread 2 shrimp onto each skewer.
Grill over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes per side until they turn pink and opaque.
Serve hot with a side of tzatziki or simple salad.

name: Lobster Tail Plate
steps: Butterfly the tail: Cut through the top of the shell lengthwise using kitchen shears. Pull the meat out and sit it on top of the shell.
Brush the meat generously with half of the melted butter mixed with paprika.
Bake or broil in the oven at 200°C for 10-12 minutes until the meat is opaque and firm.
Mix the remaining butter with lemon juice and parsley.
Serve the hot lobster tail with the lemon-butter sauce for dipping.

name: Tofu Plate (Pan-Seared)
steps: IMPORTANT: Press the tofu between paper towels and heavy plates for 15 minutes to remove water.
Dust each slab of tofu lightly with cornstarch. This is the key to a crispy exterior.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry the tofu for 4-5 minutes per side until deeply golden and crispy.
Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, and sriracha. Pour the mixture into the pan for the last 30 seconds of cooking.
Flip the tofu to coat in the sauce until it reduces and sticks to the slabs.

name: Tempeh Plate (Smoky Balsamic)
steps: Steam the tempeh slices for 10 minutes before marinating. This removes the natural bitterness and allows the marinade to penetrate deeper.
In a shallow dish, whisk together balsamic vinegar, liquid smoke, maple syrup, and garlic powder.
Place the warm tempeh in the marinade and let it sit for at least 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the tempeh for 4-5 minutes per side until the edges are crispy and dark brown.
Pour any remaining marinade into the pan for the last 30 seconds to glaze the slices.
Serve with a side of steamed kale or grains.

name: Seitan Plate (Savory Mushroom Gravy)
steps: Sear the seitan slices in a hot skillet with oil until golden brown on both sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté the mushrooms until they release their moisture and turn deep brown.
Stir in the fresh thyme and garlic. Pour in the vegetable broth and soy sauce.
Whisk the cornstarch with a tablespoon of cold water and stir it into the simmering broth.
Return the seitan to the pan. Simmer for 2-3 minutes until the gravy is thick and glossy.
Serve immediately, ideal with mashed potatoes or roasted root vegetables.

name: Vegetarian Protein Plate (Halloumi & Lentils)
steps: In a dry skillet over medium-high heat, sear the halloumi slices for 2 minutes per side until a golden crust forms. Halloumi doesn't melt, making it perfect for grilling.
Toss the warm cooked lentils with the lemon vinaigrette and roasted peppers.
Sauté the spinach in the halloumi pan until just wilted.
Arrange the lentils as the base of the plate.
Place the spinach on one side and the grilled halloumi on top.
Finish with a crack of black pepper. The saltiness of the cheese balances perfectly with the earthy lentils.

name: Vegan Protein Plate (Power Trio)
steps: Pan-fry the tofu cubes with a dash of nutritional yeast and turmeric until crispy.
Mix the cooked quinoa and black beans together; season with lime juice and cumin.
Assemble the plate: Quinoa-bean mix on one side, crispy tofu on the other.
Top with avocado slices and a sprinkle of pumpkin seeds for added protein and healthy fats.
Drizzle with a creamy tahini or lime-cilantro dressing.
This plate provides a complete amino acid profile from multiple plant sources.

name: Keto Protein Plate (Steak & Eggs)
steps: Sear the steak in a cast-iron skillet over high heat for 3-4 minutes per side. Baste with 1 tbsp of butter and garlic.
Remove the steak and let it rest for 5 minutes (essential for Keto to keep fats in the meat).
In the same pan, fry the eggs sunny-side up in the remaining steak fat.
Quickly sauté the asparagus in the pan juices until tender-crisp.
Serve the steak with the eggs on top, accompanied by avocado slices.
This plate is designed for high fat and high protein with near-zero carbohydrates.

name: Low Carb Protein Plate (Chicken & Greens)
steps: Grill the chicken breast and slice it into medallions.
Flash-sauté the zucchini noodles in a pan with a drop of oil for only 2 minutes (longer makes them watery).
Toss the hot noodles with pesto sauce and halved cherry tomatoes.
Top the noodles with the grilled chicken.
Garnish with toasted pine nuts and a sprinkle of parmesan if allowed.
A perfect low-insulin response meal that is high in volume but low in carbs.

name: High Protein Plate (Turkey & Quinoa)
steps: Brown the turkey in a skillet with garlic powder and a touch of ginger.
Add the pre-cooked quinoa to the skillet and toss with liquid aminos for savory depth.
Steam the broccoli until bright green and tender.
Assemble the plate with the turkey-quinoa mix, broccoli, and the halved hard-boiled egg.
This plate focuses on diverse protein sources (poultry, grain, egg) for a full amino acid spectrum.

name: Fitness Meal Plate (Cod & Sweet Potato)
steps: Roast sweet potato cubes at 200°C for 20 minutes until soft.
Season the cod with salt and lemon zest. Bake at the same temperature for 12-15 minutes in a separate dish.
Blanch green beans for 3 minutes and shock in cold water.
Assemble the plate: white fish for lean protein, sweet potato for complex carbs, and green beans for fiber.
Drizzle the fish with a tiny amount of olive oil and fresh lemon juice.

name: Bodybuilding Meal Plate
steps: Grill the chicken breast using only dry spices (no sugar-based rubs) to keep it clean.
Steam a large volume of broccoli to increase satiety without adding calories.
Use pre-cooked brown rice for consistent slow-release energy.
Drizzle the olive oil over the cooked rice and broccoli after serving.
This is the standard 'Clean Bulk' meal for muscle maintenance and growth.

name: Mediterranean Protein Plate (Lamb & Hummus)
steps: Grill the lamb sirloin cubes on skewers until medium-rare (about 8-10 mins).
Spread the hummus in a circular motion on half of the plate.
Place the lamb skewers on top of the hummus.
Add a pile of fresh tabbouleh and a dollop of tzatziki on the other side.
Serve with warm pita bread. This plate provides protein, probiotics, and healthy fiber.

name: Asian Protein Plate (Salmon & Edamame)
steps: Sear the salmon skin-side down until crispy, then glaze with a teaspoon of soy sauce and mirin.
Steam the edamame beans and sprinkle with flaky sea salt.
Place the salmon as the centerpiece.
Arrange edamame, seaweed salad, and ginger in neat sections around the fish.
Serve with a small warm bowl of miso soup to aid digestion.
High in Omega-3s and lean proteins.

name: Latin Protein Plate (Carne Asada & Beans)
steps: Grill the marinated flank steak over high heat for 4 minutes per side. Slice thinly against the grain.
Heat the black beans with a pinch of cumin and epazote (if available).
Arrange the sliced steak over a bed of cilantro lime rice.
Serve the beans in a small side bowl or directly on the plate.
Top with fresh Pico de Gallo and the charred jalapeño.
Rich in iron, protein, and complex carbohydrates.
equipment: Outdoor Grill or Cast Iron Skillet

name: African Protein Plate (Grilled Chicken & Yam)
steps: Marinate chicken quarters with ginger, garlic, and bouillon. Grill until the skin is charred and crispy.
Peel and cube the white yam. Deep fry until the exterior is crunchy and the interior is fluffy.
Place the grilled chicken at the center of the plate.
Arrange the hot fried yams on the side.
Add a generous scoop of Shito sauce for dipping the chicken and yams.
Balance the heavy protein with a fresh side salad.

name: Crispy Fish (Pan-Fried Whole)
steps: Clean and score the fish diagonally. Pat completely dry with paper towels.
Dredge the fish in cornstarch, ensuring it gets into the scores. Shake off excess.
Heat oil in a large skillet. Fry the fish for 6-7 minutes per side until the skin is 'shatter-crisp'.
In a small pan, flash-fry minced garlic and ginger until golden.
Pour the crispy aromatics over the fish before serving.
Serve with a soy-lime dipping sauce.

name: Middle Eastern Protein Plate (Mixed Grill)
steps: Grill the kofta and chicken skewers until well-charred and cooked through.
Deep fry or air-fry the falafel until crispy.
Arrange the skewers and falafel on a bed of yellow rice or flatbread.
Add a scoop of smoky baba ganoush (eggplant dip) to the plate.
Garnish with pink pickled turnips and fresh parsley.
This is a high-protein feast combining grilled meats and plant-based legumes.

name: Vegetable Stir Fry
steps: Prepare all vegetables beforehand; stir-frying happens too fast to chop during the process.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, cornstarch, and a splash of water to create the 'slurry'.
Heat a wok over high heat until it starts to smoke. Add 1 tablespoon of high-smoke point oil.
Toss in the carrots and baby corn first (they take longer). Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the snow peas, bok choy, and water chestnuts. Toss vigorously for 2 minutes until the greens are vibrant.
Create a space in the center, add garlic and ginger for 30 seconds, then pour the sauce slurry over the vegetables.
Toss for one final minute until the sauce thickens into a glossy coat. Serve immediately.
equipment: Wok or Large Stainless Steel Skillet

name: Pork Stir Fry
steps: Velvet the pork: Toss pork slices with egg white, cornstarch, and rice wine. Let sit for 15 minutes. This creates a protective barrier that keeps the pork tender.
Heat oil in a wok until very hot. Sear the pork in batches to avoid crowding; remove once it changes color.
Add onions and bell peppers to the wok. Stir-fry for 3 minutes until slightly charred.
Add the black bean sauce and a splash of water.
Return the pork to the wok. Toss rapidly over high heat so the savory sauce emulsifies with the pork juices.
Serve hot with steamed white rice.

name: Asian Sauté (Mushroom & Garlic)
steps: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add oil and the smashed garlic cloves.
Once garlic is fragrant and slightly brown, add all the mushrooms.
Do not stir the mushrooms for the first 3 minutes. Letting them sit allows them to caramelize and develop a meaty flavor.
Add the butter and soy sauce. Toss the mushrooms to coat.
Continue sautéing for 3 more minutes until the mushrooms have shrunk and are deep golden.
Remove the whole garlic cloves. Garnish with chopped scallions and serve as a side or topping.

name: Ginger Stir Fry
steps: Slice the ginger into very fine matchsticks. This ensures you get a spicy bite without it being overwhelming.
Sear the chicken in a smoking hot wok with oil for 3 minutes. Remove chicken.
Add the ginger matchsticks to the wok. Fry for 1 minute until crispy and fragrant.
Add garlic and bell peppers; stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Return chicken to the pan and add the oyster sauce and a tablespoon of broth or water.
Toss for 1 minute until the ginger is well distributed and the sauce is bubbling and thick.

name: Sesame Stir Fry
steps: Toss chicken cubes in cornstarch until heavily coated. Shake off excess.
Shallow fry the chicken in a skillet with 1/2 inch of oil until crispy and golden. Drain on paper towels.
Wipe the skillet and add soy sauce, honey, and sesame oil. Bring to a simmer.
Add the crispy chicken back to the skillet. Toss quickly to glaze—do not let it sit too long or the coating will get soggy.
Immediately sprinkle with a generous amount of toasted sesame seeds.
Serve over steamed broccoli and rice.

name: Sweet and Sour Stir Fry
steps: Whisk vinegar, ketchup, and sugar to make the sweet and sour base.
Sear the protein in a hot wok and remove.
Add pineapple, onions, and cucumbers. Stir-fry for 3 minutes. The cucumber adds a traditional refreshing crunch.
Pour in the sauce and bring to a boil.
Add the protein back and toss until the sauce reduces to a sticky, bright red glaze.
Serve hot; the balance of acid from vinegar and sweetness from pineapple is key.

name: Teriyaki Stir Fry
steps: Combine soy sauce, mirin, and grated ginger. Marinate protein for 10 minutes.
Sear the protein in a hot wok. Remove.
Steam-fry the vegetables with a splash of water in the wok until tender-crisp.
Return the protein and add any remaining marinade.
Toss on high heat until the sauce thickens and glazes the ingredients beautifully.
The mirin provides the signature glossy shine of Teriyaki.

name: Spicy Stir Fry (Sichuan Style)
steps: Heat oil in a wok. Add Sichuan peppercorns and dried chilies. Fry until chilies darken (do not burn).
Add the chili bean paste and fry until the oil turns bright red.
Add the protein and sear until coated in the spicy oil.
Toss in the celery and leeks; stir-fry for 3 minutes over high heat.
The dish should be 'dry-fried', meaning very little liquid sauce, just spicy infused oil coating everything.
Serve with plenty of steamed rice to balance the numbing spice (Mala).

name: Wok-fried Vegetables
steps: Blanch the Gai Lan in boiling water for 1 minute, then drain. This ensures the thick stems are cooked through.
Heat a wok until smoking. Add 2 tbsp of oil and the minced garlic.
Immediately add the blanched Gai Lan.
Pour in the oyster sauce and sugar. Toss for 60 seconds over extreme heat.
The result should be slightly charred leaves and tender-crisp stems with a savory glaze.

name: Sautéed Greens
steps: Heat olive oil in a very large skillet or pot over medium heat.
Add garlic slices and chili flakes. Cook for 2 minutes until garlic is pale golden.
Add the greens in batches (they will look like too much but will wilt significantly).
Toss with tongs until all greens are wilted and coated in the garlic oil.
Season with salt and a generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice right before serving to keep the greens bright.

name: Cashew Stir Fry
steps: Sear the chicken in a wok until cooked through. Remove.
Stir-fry the peppers and dried chilies for 2 minutes.
Add the roasted cashews to the wok. Frying them briefly in the oil enhances their nuttiness.
Return the chicken and add the soy-honey sauce.
Toss for 1 minute until the chicken and nuts are sticky and glazed.
The contrast between the soft chicken and crunchy cashews is the hallmark of this dish.

name: Healthy Protein Plate (Tofu & Sautéed Veg)
steps: Ensure the tofu is well-pressed to remove moisture.
Sauté the tofu cubes in a non-stick pan until all sides are golden and firm.
Add the mushrooms and broccoli to the pan. Sauté for 5 minutes with a splash of water to steam-fry.
Pour in the liquid aminos and garlic powder.
Toss everything together until the liquid has evaporated and coated the protein.
Serve as a clean, high-protein meal garnished with sesame seeds.

name: Grilled Fish Plate (Sautéed Sea Bass)
steps: Sear the fish fillet in a pan skin-side down for 4 minutes until crispy. Flip and cook for 2 minutes. Remove.
In the same pan, add the cherry tomatoes and capers. Sauté for 3 minutes until tomatoes burst.
Add the spinach and toss for 60 seconds until just wilted.
Place the sautéed vegetables on a plate as a bed.
Sit the fish on top of the vegetables.
Drizzle the remaining pan juices over the fish for a light, healthy sauce.

name: Broccoli Stir Fry
steps: Heat oil in a wok until it smokes.
Add the broccoli florets and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add a splash of water and immediately cover the wok with a lid for 2 minutes. This steams the broccoli so it's tender but still has a bite.
Remove lid, add garlic and chili flakes. Sauté for 1 minute.
Finish with soy sauce and sesame oil. Toss for 30 seconds and serve hot.

name: Peanut Sauce Stir Fry
steps: Whisk peanut butter, coconut milk, curry paste, and lime juice together to make the peanut sauce.
Stir-fry the protein in a hot wok until fully cooked.
Add your choice of vegetables (carrots, peas) and sauté for 2 minutes.
Pour the peanut sauce over the ingredients.
Toss gently over medium heat until the sauce is bubbly and thickens. Do not use high heat as the peanut butter can burn easily.
Serve garnished with crushed peanuts and cilantro.

name: Hoisin Stir Fry
steps: Toss the beef slices with rice vinegar and cornstarch; let it sit for 10 minutes to tenderize.
Heat a wok until it smokes. Add oil and sear the beef in a single layer until deep brown. Remove.
Add the onions to the hot wok. Stir-fry for 2 minutes until the edges are slightly charred.
Add the minced garlic and hoisin sauce. Pour in a splash of water to loosen the thick sauce.
Return the beef to the wok. Toss rapidly over high heat for 60 seconds until the sauce becomes a dark, glossy glaze that clings to the meat.
Serve immediately, ideally paired with steamed jasmine rice.

name: Garlic Sauce Stir Fry
steps: Whisk broth, soy sauce, and honey in a bowl to create the garlic sauce base.
Stir-fry the chicken in a hot wok until fully cooked. Set aside.
Blanch the broccoli in boiling water for 1 minute, then add it to the hot wok.
Add the massive amount of minced garlic and fry for only 20 seconds to prevent burning.
Pour the sauce base over the broccoli and chicken.
Toss over high heat until the garlic aroma is intense and the sauce has reduced to a thick coating.

name: Oyster Sauce Stir Fry
steps: Heat oil in a wok. Sauté the ginger matchsticks until golden.
Add the beef and sear over extreme heat. Deglaze with Shaoxing wine.
Add the snap peas and stir-fry for 90 seconds to keep them crispy.
Pour in the oyster sauce and toss vigorously.
The saltiness of the oyster sauce acts as the primary seasoning, creating a savory umami finish.
Serve with a dash of white pepper.

name: Stir-fried Protein and Veggies
steps: Chop all vegetables into uniform sizes to ensure they cook at the same rate.
Sear the chicken first, then add the shrimp for the final minute of protein cooking. Remove.
Stir-fry the vegetables in batches, starting with the hard carrots and ending with the soft peppers.
Combine everything in the pan, add the soy sauce and sesame oil.
Toss for 2 minutes to meld the flavors together.

name: Quick Stir Fry
steps: Heat a large skillet with 1 tbsp of oil until very hot.
Add the frozen vegetables directly to the pan. Cook for 5 minutes, allowing any water to evaporate.
Add the pre-cooked chicken to heat through.
Pour in the sauce and toss for 2 minutes until steaming.
Perfect for an under-15-minute meal.

name: Meal Prep Stir Fry
steps: Stir-fry the chicken in two batches to ensure a good sear rather than steaming.
Steam-fry the broccoli and cauliflower with a splash of water and a lid for 3 minutes.
Toss everything with tamari and a little ginger.
Divide the mixture into 4 airtight containers over a bed of brown rice.
Let cool completely before sealing to prevent the vegetables from turning mushy.

name: Street Wok (Beef Chow Fun)
steps: Sear the beef in a smoking hot wok and set aside.
Add the noodles to the wok. Do not stir too much to avoid breaking them.
Add the soy sauces. Use a tossing motion to color the noodles evenly without a spatula if possible.
Add the bean sprouts and return the beef.
Toss for 60 seconds over high heat for the signature smoky 'Wok Hei' flavor.
The noodles should have charred edges and look slightly oily.

name: Kitchen Wok Dish (Dry-Fried Green Beans)
steps: Dry-fry the green beans in 2 tbsp of oil over medium-high heat until the skins are wrinkled and blistered. Remove.
In the same wok, fry the ground pork until crispy.
Add the preserved vegetables and dried chilies. Sauté until fragrant.
Return the beans to the wok. Add a splash of soy sauce and sugar.
Toss until the beans are coated in the savory pork bits.

name: Red Curry (Gaeng Daeng)
