steps: Spoon the thick cream from the top of the coconut milk can into a pot. Fry over medium heat until the oil separates (cracking the cream).
Add the red curry paste and fry for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the chicken and stir to coat. Pour in the rest of the coconut milk.
Add bamboo shoots and simmer for 15 minutes.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar. Stir in fresh Thai basil at the very end and remove from heat.

name: Green Curry (Gaeng Keow Wan)
steps: Fry the green curry paste in coconut cream until the green oil rises to the surface.
Add the protein and kaffir lime leaves. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add the remaining coconut milk and Thai eggplants.
Simmer for 10-12 minutes until eggplants are soft but hold their shape.
Balance the heat with a little extra palm sugar if needed. Serve with jasmine rice.

name: Yellow Curry (Gaeng Karee)
steps: Simmer potatoes in water for 10 minutes until parboiled. Drain.
Fry the yellow curry paste in a little coconut milk.
Add the chicken, parboiled potatoes, and the rest of the coconut milk.
Simmer for 15-20 minutes until the sauce is thick and potatoes are creamy.
This curry is milder and sweeter than red or green. Garnish with crispy fried shallots.

name: Massaman Curry
steps: Sear the beef cubes until browned and set aside.
Fry the Massaman paste with coconut cream and whole spices (cinnamon/star anise).
Return the beef to the pot, add coconut milk, and simmer on low for 40 minutes until beef is tender.
Add potatoes and peanuts during the last 20 minutes.
The result should be a rich, nutty, and fragrant stew with a thick sauce.

name: Panang Curry
steps: Panang is a 'dry' curry with very little sauce. Fry the paste in coconut cream until very thick.
Add the meat and cook until done.
Stir in the crushed peanuts to thicken and flavor the sauce further.
Season with fish sauce and palm sugar.
Serve garnished with a mountain of finely shredded kaffir lime leaves for a citrusy aroma.

name: Indian Curry (Madras Style)
steps: Sauté onions until deep golden brown. Add the ginger-garlic paste and curry leaves.
Add the meat and brown it on all sides.
Stir in the curry powder and fry for 1 minute (blooming the spices).
Add tomato puree and a little water. Simmer on low for 30-40 minutes until the meat is tender and the oil separates from the sauce.
Finish with fresh coriander and serve with basmati rice.

name: Butter Chicken Style
steps: Melt butter and simmer with tomato passata and ginger-garlic paste for 10 minutes.
Add pre-grilled chicken pieces to the sauce.
Pour in the heavy cream and stir until the sauce is velvety and orange.
Add a touch of honey for sweetness and the crushed dried fenugreek for that authentic restaurant aroma.
Simmer for 5 minutes and serve with garlic naan.

name: Tikka Masala Style
steps: Marinate the protein in yogurt, lemon juice, and half of the spices for at least 2 hours.
Thread onto skewers and grill at 220°C for 15 minutes until charred (this creates the 'Tikka').
In a pot, sauté onions until golden. Add ginger-garlic paste and the remaining spices.
Pour in tomato passata and a splash of water. Simmer for 10 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Stir in the heavy cream and the grilled Tikka pieces.
Simmer for 5 minutes, allowing the smoky flavor of the meat to infuse into the creamy sauce. Garnish with fresh cilantro.

name: Lamb Curry (Rogan Josh Style)
steps: Sear the lamb cubes in hot oil or ghee until browned on all sides. Remove.
In the same pot, fry whole cardamom and cloves for 30 seconds.
Add finely chopped onions and cook until they are deep brown and jammy.
Stir in the chili, fennel, and ginger powders. Add a splash of water to prevent burning.
Return the lamb to the pot. Whisk the yogurt and stir it in slowly to prevent curdling.
Cover and simmer on very low heat for 1.5 hours until the lamb is fork-tender and the oil separates on top.

name: Beef Curry
steps: Season beef with salt and black pepper. Brown the meat in a heavy pot and set aside.
Sauté onions, mustard seeds, and curry leaves until the seeds pop.
Stir in tomato paste and curry powder; fry for 2 minutes to 'bloom' the spices.
Add the beef back with 2 cups of water. Simmer on low for 1.5 to 2 hours.
Once the beef is tender, stir in the coconut milk for richness.
Simmer uncovered for 10 minutes to reduce the sauce to a thick, dark gravy.

name: Vegetable Curry
steps: Sauté onions and a pinch of salt until softened. Add ginger and garlic.
Add the potatoes and cauliflower; fry for 5 minutes until they start to color.
Add the spices and toss to coat the vegetables.
Pour in the coconut milk and simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Fold in the green peas at the last minute to keep them bright and sweet.
Garnish with cilantro and serve with steamed rice.

name: Chickpea Curry (Chana Masala)
steps: Sauté onions until brown. Add the slit green chilies and ginger-garlic paste.
Add the tomato puree and spices. Cook until the oil begins to separate from the tomato base.
Add the rinsed chickpeas and a cup of water.
Use a spoon to mash a few chickpeas against the side of the pot; this thickens the gravy naturally.
Simmer for 15 minutes. Stir in the Amchur powder at the end for a traditional tangy finish.

name: Lentil Curry (Dal Tadka)

ingredients.name: Yellow Moong Dal or Red Lentils

steps: Boil the lentils with 3 cups of water and turmeric until completely soft and mushy.
Whisk the lentils until they reach a creamy consistency. Season with salt.
Prepare the 'Tadka' (Tempering): Heat ghee in a small pan until smoking.
Add cumin seeds, dried chilies, and garlic slices. Fry until the garlic is golden and aromatic.
Pour the sizzling ghee mixture immediately over the lentils. Cover the pot for 2 minutes to trap the smoke.
Stir gently and serve hot with rice or flatbread.

name: Coconut Curry (South Indian Style)
steps: In a pan, sizzle mustard seeds and curry leaves in coconut oil.
Add shallots and sauté until translucent. Stir in spices and tamarind.
Pour in the coconut milk and bring to a very gentle simmer.
Add the seafood. Cook for only 3-5 minutes until the protein is just done.
Do not boil the coconut milk rapidly as it may split. Serve with appam or steamed rice.
equipment: Shallow Pan or Clay Pot (Meen Satti)

name: Spicy Curry (Vindaloo)
steps: Blend dried chilies, ginger, garlic, cinnamon, and cloves with vinegar to make a thick, spicy paste.
Marinate the meat in this paste for at least 4 hours (overnight is best).
Fry onions in oil until caramelized. Add the marinated meat and fry for 10 minutes.
Add a little water, cover, and simmer for 45 minutes until the meat is tender and the sauce is a deep, fiery red.
The vinegar acts as a preservative and provides the signature 'sharp' heat of an authentic Vindaloo.

name: Mild Curry (Korma Style)
steps: Blend the soaked nuts with a little milk to create a smooth, white paste.
Sauté onions in white butter or ghee until translucent (do not brown them).
Add the chicken and white spices. Cook until chicken is opaque.
Stir in the nut paste and the remaining milk. Simmer on low heat.
Add saffron-infused water for a delicate aroma.
The result should be a pale, rich, and creamy curry with zero chili heat.

name: Thai Curry Bowl
steps: Flash-fry the curry paste in a little oil until fragrant.
Add coconut milk and bring to a simmer. Add the protein and cook for 5 minutes.
In a deep bowl, place a large scoop of jasmine rice on one side.
Ladle the hot curry into the other side of the bowl.
Garnish the bowl with fresh basil leaves and a lime wedge.
This 'Bowl' style is a popular modern presentation for a balanced lunch.

name: Indian Curry Bowl
steps: Prepare a quick Makhani (butter) sauce by simmering tomato puree with butter and cream.
Add pre-cooked protein to the sauce to heat through.
Assemble the bowl: Rice at the bottom, curry on top.
Add a dollop of cold cucumber raita on the side to balance the spice.
Top with pickled onions and serve as a complete meal-in-a-bowl.

name: African Stew (Mafe Peanut Stew)
steps: Brown the meat in a heavy pot. Add onions and sauté until soft.
Add the tomato paste and fry for 5 minutes (darkening the paste is key).
Whisk the peanut butter with a cup of warm water and pour it into the pot.
Add the root vegetables and cabbage.
Simmer for 30-40 minutes on medium-low. The peanut oil will rise to the top when it's done.
Serve over white rice or with Fufu.

name: Lamb Stew (Irish Style)
steps: Sear the lamb chunks in a pot until browned. Remove.
Layer the vegetables at the bottom of the pot, then place the lamb on top.
Pour in the broth and add the thyme. Do not stir too much to keep the vegetables from breaking.
Simmer on very low heat for 1.5 hours.
The starch from the potatoes will naturally thicken the stew into a hearty consistency.
Finish with a handful of fresh parsley.

name: Fish Stew (Brazilian Moqueca)

ingredients.name: Dendê Oil (Palm Oil) or Olive Oil

steps: Marinate fish chunks in lime juice, garlic, and salt for 15 minutes.
Layer the sliced onions and peppers at the bottom of a wide pan.
Place the fish chunks on top of the vegetables.
Pour the coconut milk and Dendê oil over everything. Do not stir.
Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes. The vegetables will steam the fish from below while the coconut milk poaches it from above.
Top with fresh cilantro and serve with white rice.

name: Seafood Stew (Cioppino Style)
steps: Sauté fennel, onions, and garlic until softened.
Deglaze the pan with white wine. Pour in the crushed tomatoes and a splash of seafood stock.
Simmer the base for 15 minutes to develop the tomato flavor.
Add the seafood in order of cooking time: first the fish, then the clams, then the shrimp.
Cover and cook for 5 minutes until clams open and shrimp are pink.
Serve with toasted sourdough bread to soak up the rich, wine-infused tomato broth.
equipment: Large Dutch Oven or Deep Skillet

name: Healthy Stew
steps: In a large pot, brown the lean ground meat over medium heat. Drain any excess fat.
Add diced onions and garlic, sautéing until fragrant and soft.
Stir in the zucchini, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth. Season with oregano, salt, and pepper.
Simmer for 20 minutes until the zucchini is tender.
Add the rinsed white beans and fresh spinach. Cook for 5 more minutes until the spinach is just wilted.
This stew is designed to be high-fiber and low-fat. Serve warm with a small piece of whole-grain bread.

name: Vegan Stew
steps: Sauté onions and ginger in olive oil until golden.
Add the Ras el Hanout spice blend and toast for 1 minute to release the oils.
Add the sweet potato cubes and chickpeas, tossing to coat them in the spices.
Pour in the vegetable stock and add the sliced dried apricots for a traditional North African sweetness.
Simmer on low heat for 35 minutes until the sweet potatoes are creamy and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in fresh cilantro and a squeeze of lemon before serving over couscous.

name: Garden Salad
steps: Wash the salad greens in very cold water. Spin them dry thoroughly—wet leaves will prevent the dressing from sticking.
In a large bowl, combine the greens, cucumber, tomatoes, carrots, and radishes.
Whisk your balsamic vinaigrette (oil, vinegar, Dijon, honey) until emulsified.
Drizzle the dressing around the sides of the bowl rather than directly on top; this allows for more even coating.
Toss gently with your hands or large tongs to ensure every leaf is lightly glistening.
Serve immediately to maintain the crunch of the vegetables.

name: Cobb Salad
steps: Place the chopped romaine at the bottom of a wide shallow bowl.
The hallmark of a Cobb salad is the presentation: Arrange the chicken, egg, bacon, avocado, blue cheese, and tomatoes in neat, separate rows on top of the lettuce.
Prepare the classic dressing by whisking olive oil, red wine vinegar, a touch of mustard, and salt.
Serve the dressing on the side or drizzle it over the rows just before serving.
To eat, the salad should be tossed thoroughly so every bite has a bit of each ingredient.

name: Green Salad
steps: Tear the butter lettuce leaves by hand into large pieces. Avoid using a knife to prevent bruising.
Make a 'French Vinaigrette': Whisk the mustard and vinegar in a bowl, then slowly drip in the oil while whisking constantly to create a stable emulsion.
Lightly season the leaves with salt and pepper before adding the dressing.
Toss the leaves very gently so as not to crush the delicate butter lettuce.
Top with snipped chives. This salad is the quintessential 'simple' side for heavy French mains.

name: Kale Salad
steps: Remove the tough center ribs from the kale and chop the leaves into bite-sized ribbons.
IMPORTANT: Place the kale in a bowl with the olive oil, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt. Massage the leaves with your hands for 2-3 minutes until they become dark, soft, and reduce in volume.
Let the massaged kale sit for 10 minutes; the acid will continue to 'cook' the tough fibers.
Add the shaved parmesan and toasted pine nuts.
Toss once more and serve. This salad is excellent for meal prep as it doesn't wilt quickly.

name: Spinach Salad
steps: Ensure the baby spinach is dry and chilled.
In a large bowl, layer the spinach with the sliced strawberries.
Add the crumbled goat cheese and candied walnuts for a mix of creamy and crunchy textures.
Drizzle with the poppy seed dressing (or a light balsamic).
Toss very lightly right before serving to prevent the spinach from becoming soggy from the acid.

name: Asian Salad
steps: Shred the napa cabbage and carrots into very fine strips using a mandoline or sharp knife.
In a large bowl, combine the cabbage, edamame, and mandarin oranges.
Add the wonton strips at the very last second to ensure they stay crunchy.
Pour the sesame ginger dressing over the salad and toss well.
Garnish with black sesame seeds for visual contrast and a nutty aroma.

name: Southwest Salad
steps: Char the corn in a dry skillet for 3 minutes to get a smoky flavor.
Place chopped romaine in a bowl.
Add the black beans, charred corn, avocado, and Pico de Gallo.
Drizzle with the cilantro lime dressing (blend cilantro, lime, garlic, and oil).
Top with a generous handful of tortilla strips for crunch and serve with a wedge of lime.

name: Arugula Salad
steps: Wash the arugula and spin it dry. It must be bone-dry to keep its peppery bite.
In a small bowl, whisk the lemon juice and olive oil with a pinch of salt.
Toss the arugula with the dressing until every leaf is lightly coated.
Transfer to a chilled plate.
Use a vegetable peeler to shave large, thin ribbons of parmesan over the top.
Finish with a heavy amount of freshly cracked black pepper.

name: Mediterranean Salad (Horiatiki)
steps: Note: Authentic Greek salad does not contain lettuce. Combine the cucumber chunks, tomato wedges, red onion, and olives in a bowl.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and a splash of red wine vinegar.
Toss to coat the vegetables.
Instead of crumbling the cheese, place the whole slab of feta directly on top of the salad.
Sprinkle the entire salad—especially the feta—with the dried oregano.
Serve at room temperature with crusty bread.

name: Potato Salad
steps: Boil the potato cubes in salted water until fork-tender but not falling apart. Drain and let them cool for 10 minutes.
While the potatoes are still slightly warm (to absorb the flavor), toss them with a tablespoon of cider vinegar.
In a separate bowl, mix the mayonnaise, mustard, diced celery, and chopped herbs.
Gently fold the dressing and chopped eggs into the potatoes.
Chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the starch and dressing to bond.

name: Quinoa Salad
steps: Rinse the quinoa thoroughly to remove the bitter saponin coating.
Boil the quinoa in 2 cups of water for 15 minutes. Drain any excess liquid and fluff with a fork. Let it cool.
In a large bowl, combine the cooled quinoa with the diced vegetables and chickpeas.
Stir in the chopped parsley and lemon vinaigrette.
This salad is very durable and is perfect for healthy lunch meal prep.

name: Egg Salad
steps: Boil the eggs for 10 minutes. Immediately submerge them in an ice-water bath for 5 minutes (this makes peeling easy).
Peel the eggs and dice them into 1cm chunks.
In a bowl, whisk the mayonnaise, mustard, and herbs.
Fold the eggs gently into the dressing so they don't turn into a paste.
Season with salt and a lot of black pepper. Serve on lettuce leaves or in a sandwich.

name: Chickpea Salad
steps: Drain and rinse the chickpeas thoroughly under cold water.
In a bowl, combine the chickpeas with diced cucumbers and onions.
Whisk together tahini, lemon juice, and a splash of warm water to make the dressing.
Toss the salad with the dressing and cumin.
Garnish with a heavy amount of fresh parsley. This protein-rich salad is best served chilled.

name: Pasta Salad
steps: Boil the pasta in heavily salted water until just past al dente (pasta firms up when cold). Drain and rinse immediately with cold water.
In a large bowl, combine the cold pasta with cherry tomatoes, mozzarella pearls, olives, and diced salami.
Pour the Italian vinaigrette over the ingredients. Toss vigorously to ensure the pasta ridges are well-coated.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes. This allows the pasta to absorb the dressing, which is essential for a non-dry salad.
Toss again before serving and garnish with fresh parsley and a sprinkle of dried oregano.

name: Fruit Salad
steps: Wash and dry all fruits thoroughly. Excess water will dilute the flavor of the dressing.
Cut the pineapple and strawberries into uniform bite-sized pieces.
In a small ramekin, whisk honey and fresh lime juice until combined.
Place all fruit in a large glass bowl. Pour the glaze over the top.
Fold gently with a spatula to avoid crushing the delicate berries.
Top with shredded mint leaves. Serve chilled. For the best texture, serve within 2 hours of preparation.

name: Healthy Bowl (Quinoa & Roasted Veg)
steps: Use warm, fluffy quinoa as the base of the bowl.
Arrange roasted sweet potatoes (seasoned with cinnamon) and crispy roasted chickpeas in separate sections.
Place a handful of raw baby spinach in the corner; the warmth of the other ingredients will slightly wilt it.
Drizzle the entire bowl with a creamy lemon-tahini-garlic dressing.
Garnish with pumpkin seeds for a boost of zinc and healthy fats.
This bowl is designed to be a balance of complex carbs, plant protein, and healthy fiber.

name: Protein Bowl (Chicken & Edamame)
steps: Layer the bottom of a deep bowl with warm brown rice.
Arrange the sliced grilled chicken on one side.
Place the steamed edamame, shredded carrots, and the halved hard-boiled egg alongside the chicken.
Add a sprinkle of black sesame seeds over the egg for a professional finish.
Pour the soy-ginger dressing over the protein components.
This bowl maximizes satiety with a high protein-to-calorie ratio.

name: Veggie Bowl (Hummus & Harvest)
steps: In a wide bowl, place a large scoop of hummus in the center and spread it slightly to create a well.
Surround the hummus with roasted cauliflower florets and warm falafel.
Add a portion of fresh diced cucumber and tomato salad seasoned with lemon.
Top the hummus well with pickled red onions.
Drizzle everything with extra virgin olive oil and a generous dusting of Za'atar spice.
Serve with a warm wedge of whole-wheat pita.

name: Grain Bowl (Farro & Beets)
steps: Boil farro in salted water until tender-chewy, then drain and cool slightly.
Roast beets at 200°C for 25 minutes until tender. Let cool and dice into cubes.
Create a bed of arugula in a bowl and top with the farro grains.
Arrange the roasted beets and toasted walnuts on top.
Sprinkle the creamy goat cheese crumbles over the bowl.
Drizzle with a thick balsamic reduction. The sweetness of the beets and acidity of the glaze balance the earthy farro.

name: Detox Bowl (Green Goddess)
steps: Lightly steam the broccoli and asparagus for only 3 minutes to preserve the nutrients and vibrant green color.
Arrange the steamed vegetables in a bowl with fresh cucumber slices and half an avocado.
The 'Green Goddess' dressing (blended spinach, herbs, lemon, and Greek yogurt) is the key to this bowl.
Pour the dressing generously over the greens.
Top with pumpkin seeds and fresh herbs.
Designed for alkalinity and digestive ease, high in chlorophyll and fiber.

name: Fitness Bowl (White Fish & Rice)
steps: Bake the fish fillet with a sprinkle of garlic powder and lemon zest at 200°C for 12 minutes.
Place a portion of steamed jasmine rice at the bottom of a clean bowl.
Add the steamed green beans on the side.
Sit the baked fish on top of the rice.
Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the fish and rice just before eating. This is a low-fat, high-absorption meal ideal for post-workout.

name: Power Bowl (Ancient Grains)
steps: Mix the cooked ancient grain blend to create a complex texture base.
Massage the kale with a little olive oil until soft and dark green.
Toss the grains with hemp seeds and slivered almonds.
Arrange the kale in the bowl and top with the grain-nut mixture.
Sprinkle the dried blueberries over the top for small bursts of sweetness.
Drizzle with the maple-dijon dressing. This bowl is designed for sustained energy and high micronutrient density.

name: Salad Meal Prep (Mason Jar Style)
steps: IMPORTANT: The order of layering is the key to preventing sogginess in meal prep.
Layer 1 (Bottom): Pour the dressing into the bottom of a large glass Mason jar.
Layer 2: Add 'hard' vegetables that can marinate in the dressing, like chickpeas, peppers, and cucumbers.
Layer 3: Add cheese or proteins.
Layer 4 (Top): Pack the salad greens loosely at the very top, furthest from the dressing.
Seal the jar and refrigerate. When ready to eat, shake the jar vigorously and pour into a bowl.

name: Bowl Meal Prep (Divided Components)
steps: Cook a large batch of grain and protein separately.
Roast a variety of vegetables (broccoli, peppers, onions) on one large baking sheet.
Divide the grains and roasted vegetables among 4 airtight meal prep containers.
Place the cooked protein on one side of each container.
CRITICAL: Keep the sauce in a separate small condiment cup within the container to prevent the rice from absorbing all the moisture during storage.
Cool completely before sealing and storing for up to 4 days.

name: Dressing on the Side Salad
steps: Wash and spin the lettuce until bone-dry using a salad spinner.
Arrange the dry lettuce, cucumbers, and radishes in a serving bowl.
Prepare a fresh vinaigrette by emulsifying oil and vinegar in a small glass jar.
Instead of tossing, serve the salad with the dressing in a separate small dish.
This method is ideal for those who prefer to control their calorie intake or for salads being transported to avoid wilting.

name: Classic Burger
steps: Form the ground beef into a loose patty about 1 inch wider than the bun (it will shrink). Season only with salt and pepper.
Heat a heavy skillet or griddle until smoking hot. Sear the patty for 3-4 minutes per side without pressing down (to keep the juices inside).
Toast the split brioche bun cut-side down in the beef fat for 1 minute.
Spread the burger sauce on the bottom bun. Layer the pickles and lettuce.
Place the hot patty on top, followed by the tomato slice.
Close the burger and let it rest for 2 minutes before serving so the juices redistribute.

name: Cheese Burger
steps: Prepare caramelized onions beforehand by slowly frying them in butter for 20 minutes.
Sear the beef patty on a hot griddle for 4 minutes.
Flip the patty and immediately place two slices of cheddar cheese on top.
Cover the pan with a lid or a metal bowl for 1 minute; the steam will melt the cheese perfectly over the edges of the meat.
Toast the bun and apply the condiments.
Assemble by placing the cheesy patty on the bun and topping with a generous mound of caramelized onions.

name: Chicken Burger
steps: Pound the chicken breast to a uniform thickness. Dip in buttermilk, then seasoned flour.
Shallow fry the chicken in a skillet with 1/2 inch of oil for 4-5 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Toast the bun until golden brown.
Spread spicy mayo on both sides of the bun.
Place the crispy chicken on the bottom bun and top with a large portion of creamy coleslaw.
The cold, crunchy slaw balances the hot, spicy chicken perfectly.

name: Veggie Burger
steps: Dry the rinsed black beans in a 150°C oven for 15 minutes. Removing moisture is the secret to a firm, meaty texture.
In a bowl, mash the beans with a fork but leave some chunks for texture. Stir in the cooked quinoa, breadcrumbs, egg, and spices.
Form the mixture into thick patties. Chill in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to allow the binders to set (cold-binding).
Heat a lightly oiled skillet over medium heat. Fry the patties for 5-6 minutes per side until a dark crust forms.
Serve on a toasted whole wheat bun with sliced avocado, sprouts, and a dollop of chipotle mayo.

name: Fish Burger
steps: Pat the fish fillet dry. Season with salt and lemon pepper.
Dredge in flour, dip in egg wash, and coat thoroughly with panko, pressing firmly to ensure the crust sticks.
Shallow fry in 1/2 inch of neutral oil over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes per side until the crust is golden and 'shatter-crisp'.
Toast the brioche bun. Spread tartar sauce on both sides.
Layer the shredded lettuce on the bottom, followed by the crispy fish. Serve immediately to maintain the crunch.

name: Slider Plate
steps: Divide the beef into three 100g balls. Flatten them into patties slightly larger than the mini buns.
Sear on a very hot griddle for 2 minutes per side. Use a heavy spatula to 'smash' them once for a crispy edge.
Top with cheese for the final 30 seconds of cooking, covering with a lid to melt.
Toast the mini buns on the same griddle to absorb the beef drippings.
Spread truffle mayo on the buns, add the patties, and top with caramelized onions. Serve as a trio on a single plate.

name: Club Sandwich
steps: Toast all three slices of bread until golden and firm.
Spread mayo on the first slice. Add turkey and lettuce.
Place the second slice of toast on top. Spread with mayo, then add bacon and tomato slices.
Spread mayo on the third slice and place it face down to close the sandwich.
Insert toothpicks into the four corners. Use a serrated knife to cut the sandwich into four triangles. The toothpicks keep the 'tower' stable while cutting.

name: BLT Sandwich
steps: Cook bacon in a cold skillet, gradually increasing heat to medium. This renders the fat perfectly for maximum crispness.
Toast sourdough in the residual bacon fat for a boost of flavor.
Season the tomato slices with salt and pepper beforehand (this is a chef's secret for a great BLT).
Generously mayo the toast. Layer the crisp lettuce, the seasoned tomatoes, and finally a dense layer of bacon.
Press down gently and cut diagonally. The ratio of bacon to tomato should be nearly 1:1.

name: Steak Sandwich
steps: Sear the steak strips over very high heat for only 2 minutes until charred but medium-rare. Let rest.
Slice the baguette lengthwise and toast the interior under a broiler.
Spread horseradish cream on the bottom piece and top with fresh arugula.
Layer the warm steak strips and the caramelized onions on top.
Pour any resting juices from the steak over the meat before closing the sandwich.
Serve warm, often paired with a side of au jus for dipping.

name: Tuna Melt
steps: Mix tuna, mayo, Dijon, and relish in a bowl until smooth.
Butter the outside of each bread slice generously.
Place one slice butter-side down in a skillet over medium heat. Layer one slice of cheese, the tuna mixture, and the second slice of cheese.
Top with the second bread slice (butter-side up).
Grill for 3-4 minutes per side. Use a lid to trap heat and ensure the cheese melts completely and the tuna is hot in the center.
The exterior should be deep golden brown and the cheese should be bubbling.

name: Chicken Wrap
steps: Warm the tortilla in a dry pan for 15 seconds so it doesn't tear during rolling.
Spread hummus in a thin layer across the center, leaving a 2-inch border at the edges.
Layer the chicken, spinach, carrots, and cucumbers in a neat line.
Fold the sides of the tortilla inward.
Start rolling from the bottom, keeping it as tight as possible (the 'Cigar' method).
Slice on a steep diagonal for a professional presentation.

name: Beef Wrap
steps: Briefly heat the beef in a pan with a splash of water to keep it moist.
Spread tahini sauce on a large tortilla or flatbread.
Add the warm beef, red onions, parsley, and pickled turnips.
Roll tightly and toast the wrapped sandwich in a dry pan for 1 minute seam-side down.
This 'tostado' finish seals the wrap and adds a necessary crunch.

name: Falafel Wrap
steps: Lightly steam the pita bread so it becomes soft and pliable.
Place the falafel balls in a row and lightly crush them with a fork to prevent them from rolling out.
Top with the diced salad and a generous drizzle of tahini and Amba.
Roll the wrap and secure the bottom with parchment paper or foil (Street Food style).
The contrast between the hot, crispy falafel and cold, tangy salad is essential.

name: Beef Burrito
steps: Warm the large tortilla on a griddle until it bubbles slightly.
Spread refried beans as the first layer (acts as glue). Add rice, beef, and cheese.
Fold the sides in, then bring the bottom over the filling and tuck it under as you roll tightly (Triple-fold).
Place the burrito seam-side down on the hot griddle for 60 seconds to 'lock' the wrap.
Serve with salsa and lime on the side.

name: Soft Taco (Carnitas style)
steps: Heat the pork carnitas in a skillet until the edges become crispy and caramelized.
Warm the corn tortillas directly over a gas flame for 10 seconds per side until they char slightly.
Double up the tortillas if they are thin. Add a heap of the crispy pork.
Garnish with raw onions and fresh cilantro.
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the top and add a spoonful of salsa verde.

name: Hard Shell Taco
steps: Bake the empty taco shells at 150°C for 5 minutes to restore their crunch and bring out the corn aroma.
Layer the hot seasoned beef at the bottom of each shell.
Immediately top with shredded cheese so it melts slightly from the heat of the meat.
Add cold lettuce, tomatoes, and a dollop of sour cream on top.
Eat immediately before the bottom of the shell absorbs moisture and becomes soft.

name: Chicken Quesadilla
steps: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat.
Place the tortilla in the pan. Sprinkle cheese over the entire surface.
Add chicken and jalapeños to only one half of the tortilla.
Once the cheese starts to melt, fold the empty half over the filling.
Cook for 3 minutes per side, pressing down with a spatula until the exterior is golden-brown and crispy.
Slice into wedges using a pizza cutter.

name: Loaded Nachos (Supreme)
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C. Spread chips in a single layer on a large baking sheet.
Top with black beans and jalapeños. Bake for 5 minutes to toast the chips and heat the beans.
Pour hot nacho cheese sauce evenly over the warm chips.
The secret is the cold topping finish: Add large scoops of cold guacamole, fresh salsa, and sour cream over the hot base.
Serve immediately in the center of the table for sharing.

name: Taco Plate (Tres Tipos)
steps: Prepare three distinct fillings: sear minced beef with cumin, sauté shredded chicken in tomato sauce, and fry chorizo until crispy.
Warm the tortillas on a flat griddle until slightly charred and flexible.
Assemble the tacos in pairs of two for each filling.
Place a scoop of warm Mexican rice and a side of refried beans topped with melted cheese on a large plate.
Arrange the six tacos in the center.
Garnish with lime wedges and serve the salsas in small side ramekins. This is a complete 'Platter' presentation.

name: Empanadas (Beef & Olive)
steps: Sauté onions and beef with cumin and paprika until fully cooked. Let the mixture cool completely (cold filling prevents the dough from getting soggy).
Stir in the chopped eggs and sliced olives.
Place a spoonful of filling in the center of each dough disc.
Fold the dough over and seal by 'crimping' the edges with a fork or using the traditional 'repulgue' (braiding) technique with your fingers.
Brush the tops with egg wash for a golden shine.
Bake at 200°C (400°F) for 15-20 minutes until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.

name: Deep Dish Pizza (Chicago Style)
steps: Generously butter a 9-inch round cake pan or deep-dish pan and sprinkle with cornmeal.
Press the dough into the bottom and 2 inches up the sides of the pan.
LAYER ORDER IS REVERSED: First, lay the mozzarella slices directly onto the dough.
Add the raw crumbled sausage and any other toppings over the cheese.
Pour the thick, chunky tomato sauce over the top, spreading it to the edges.
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
Bake at 220°C (425°F) for 30-35 minutes. Let it rest for 5 minutes in the pan before slicing so the cheese doesn't run out.
equipment: 9-inch Deep-Dish Pizza Pan or Round Cake Pan

name: Flatbread Pizza
steps: Place the flatbreads on a baking sheet and bake for 3 minutes at 200°C to crisp the base.
Spread a thin layer of pesto or marinara.
Add caramelized onions and goat cheese crumbles.
Return to the oven for 5-7 minutes until the cheese is soft and the edges are golden.
Remove and immediately top with fresh cold arugula.
Drizzle with balsamic glaze and slice into rectangles.

name: Artisan Pizza (Sourdough Base)
steps: Preheat a pizza stone at 260°C (500°F) for at least one hour.
Dust a pizza peel with semolina. Gently stretch the dough by hand (do not use a rolling pin) to preserve the air bubbles in the crust.
Add a very light layer of crushed tomatoes and the buffalo mozzarella.
Slide onto the hot stone and bake for 6-8 minutes until the crust has large charred 'leopard spots'.
Remove from oven and drape the raw prosciutto over the hot pizza.
Finish with a drizzle of truffle oil or fresh honey if using figs. The contrast of the hot crust and cold raw ham is the artisan signature.

name: Gourmet Pizza (White Truffle & Mushroom)
steps: Sauté the wild mushrooms in butter and thyme until browned and all water is evaporated.
Stretch the dough and spread the ricotta-mascarpone mixture as the base (no tomato sauce).
Distribute the mushrooms and fontina cheese evenly.
Bake until the crust is blistered and the cheese is bubbling.
Remove and immediately drizzle with the white truffle oil while the pizza is steaming to release the aroma.
Slice and serve with a glass of dry white wine.

name: Fresh Pita Bread
steps: Mix water, sugar, and yeast; let it foam. Knead with flour and oil for 10 minutes until elastic.
Let rise in a warm bowl for 1 hour until doubled in size.
Divide into 8 balls and roll into 6-inch circles about 0.5cm thick.
Heat a cast-iron skillet over high heat until it smokes slightly.
Place dough circle in the dry pan. After 30 seconds, bubbles will form; flip it.
The pita should puff up like a balloon in the next minute (this creates the pocket).
Remove and keep in a clean towel to stay soft.

name: Homemade Naan Bread
steps: Knead flour, yogurt, yeast, and warm water into a soft, slightly sticky dough.
Let it rise for 2 hours. The yogurt is the secret to the soft, chewy texture.
Divide into 6 balls. Roll into teardrop shapes.
Heat a griddle or pizza stone to maximum heat.
Press nigella seeds into the surface of the dough.
Place on the hot surface. Once large charred bubbles appear (approx 2 mins), flip and cook for 1 minute.
Immediately brush with hot ghee and stack under a towel.

name: Garlic Flatbread
steps: Roll out the dough into a long, rustic oval shape.
In a small bowl, combine melted butter, minced garlic, and parsley.
Brush half the garlic butter over the raw dough.
Bake at 220°C (425°F) for 8-10 minutes until the edges are golden and crispy.
Remove from oven and immediately brush with the remaining garlic butter and a sprinkle of sea salt.
Cut into strips and serve warm as an appetizer.

name: Fluffy Buttermilk Pancakes
steps: Whisk dry ingredients in one bowl and wet ingredients in another.
Gently combine; the batter should remain lumpy. Over-mixing will make the pancakes tough.
Heat a non-stick griddle over medium heat. Lightly grease with a tiny bit of butter.
Pour 1/4 cup of batter per pancake.
Wait for bubbles to form on the surface AND the edges to look set before flipping (about 3 mins).
Cook for another 2 minutes until golden brown. Serve in a stack with a knob of butter and warm syrup.

name: Belgian Waffles
steps: SECRET FOR FLUFFINESS: Separate the egg whites from the yolks. Beat the egg whites in a clean bowl until stiff peaks form.
Mix yolks, milk, oil, and dry ingredients in a large bowl until smooth.
Gently fold the stiff egg whites into the batter with a spatula to trap the air.
Pour onto a preheated, greased waffle iron.
Cook for 4-5 minutes until the steam stops rising and the waffle is deep golden and crisp.
Serve with fresh berries and whipped cream.

name: Classic French Toast
steps: Whisk eggs, milk, cinnamon, and vanilla in a shallow dish.
Soak brioche slices for 1 minute per side until they have absorbed the custard to the center.
Heat butter in a skillet over medium-low heat until foamy.
Fry the bread for 4 minutes per side. Low and slow is key to ensure the center cooks without burning the exterior sugar.
The result should be 'custard-like' in the middle and golden on the outside.
Dust with powdered sugar and serve with bacon.

name: Breakfast Plate (Classic American)
steps: Start the bacon in a cold skillet; cook over medium heat until crispy. Set aside on paper towels.
In the residual bacon fat, fry the hash browns until deeply golden and crispy on both sides.
Wipe the pan and melt a little butter. Fry the eggs to the diner's preference (Over-easy or Sunny-side up).
Toast the bread and butter it while hot.
Arrange the eggs, bacon, and potatoes neatly on a large plate with the toast. A classic start to the day.

name: Egg Plate (International Style)
steps: Boil eggs for exactly 6 minutes and 30 seconds for a perfect 'jammy' yolk. Shock in ice water immediately.
Arrange the cold ham, salami, and cheese slices in a fanned-out pattern on one half of the plate.
Place the warm croissant or roll on the other side.
Peel the eggs carefully and slice in half; place on the plate with a pinch of sea salt.
Add a small selection of grapes or apple slices for a fresh finish.
Serve with jam and butter on the side.

name: Classic Fried Eggs
steps: Melt butter in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat until just bubbling.
Crack eggs gently into the pan. Do not overcrowd.
For 'Sunny Side Up': Cook for 3 minutes until the whites are set but the yolk is liquid. Do not flip.
For 'Over Easy': Cook for 2 minutes, flip gently with a silicone spatula, and cook for 30 seconds more.
The key is low heat to avoid 'browned' or rubbery edges on the egg whites.
Season with salt and pepper right before serving.

name: Breakfast Plate (Bistro Style)
steps: Roast cherry tomatoes in a pan with a drop of oil and salt until the skins burst.
Toast the sourdough until golden; spread a thin layer of cream cheese on it.
Prepare the eggs as 'Over Easy' or 'Poached' according to preference.
Arrange the salmon slices in a rosette pattern on the plate.
Add the sliced avocado and roasted tomatoes.
Place the toast on the side. This plate provides a balanced mix of healthy fats, protein, and complex carbs.

name: Egg Plate (Protein-Packed)
steps: Grill the turkey sausages in a skillet until browned and cooked through.
Wilt the spinach in the same pan with a pinch of garlic powder.
Prepare the eggs as a soft scramble or sunny-side up.
Sprinkle crumbled feta over the hot eggs.
Serve everything alongside a toasted whole wheat English muffin for a high-protein start to the day.

name: Fried Eggs (Perfect Crispy Edge)
steps: Heat butter in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat until it starts to foam and turn slightly brown.
Crack eggs gently into the pan. The high heat will create the 'lacy' crispy brown edges.
Immediately spoon some of the hot butter over the egg whites (but not the yolks) to help them set quickly.
Cook for 3 minutes for a runny yolk. If 'Over Easy' is desired, flip carefully and cook for only 30 seconds more.
Slide onto a plate and season with salt and pepper.

name: Vegetarian Breakfast (Garden spread)
steps: Sauté mushrooms and asparagus in olive oil until tender.
Sear halloumi in a dry pan for 2 minutes per side until golden.
Heat the baked beans in a small saucepan.
Poach an egg in simmering water for 3.5 minutes.
Assemble the plate with the halloumi as the savory 'meat' substitute.
Place the poached egg over the asparagus and serve hot.

name: Vegan Breakfast (Tofu Scramble)
steps: Sauté onions and peppers in a pan until soft.
Crumble the tofu by hand into the pan.
Add nutritional yeast, turmeric, and black salt. The black salt provides the sulfurous aroma of real eggs.
Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring frequently, until the tofu is heated through and slightly golden.
Serve with avocado toast and vegan sausage.

name: Keto Breakfast (Salmon & Fat-Bombs)
steps: Slice the avocado in half and remove the pit. Fill the center with a little olive oil and lemon juice.
Slice the hard-boiled eggs in half and season with salt and hemp seeds.
Arrange the smoked salmon slices neatly next to the avocado.
This breakfast is near-zero carbohydrate and extremely high in healthy Omega-3 fats and protein.
Finish with a sprinkle of red pepper flakes for metabolism boost.

name: Low Calorie Breakfast (Egg White Power)
steps: Sauté mushrooms and spinach in a non-stick pan with a light spray of oil.
Pour the egg whites over the vegetables.
Cook over low heat, gently folding until the whites are opaque and set.
Serve with a side of fresh salsa to provide flavor without added fat or sugar.
Ideal for those on a strict calorie-deficit diet.

name: Healthy Breakfast (Oat & Fruit)
steps: Simmer oats in almond milk over medium heat for 10 minutes until creamy.
Stir in the chia seeds halfway through; they will absorb liquid and add thickness.
Transfer to a bowl.
Top with fresh blueberries and crushed walnuts for antioxidants and healthy brain fats.
Drizzle with a tiny amount of honey if extra sweetness is needed.

name: Full English Breakfast
steps: Start the sausages first in a large skillet; they take the longest (15 mins).
Add the bacon and black pudding to the same pan after 5 minutes.
Halve the tomato and sauté it cut-side down until charred. Fry the whole mushroom.
Heat the beans in a separate small pot.
Fry the eggs last in the rendered fat from the bacon and sausages.
Fry the bread in the pan until golden and crispy.
Assemble on a massive plate. Everything should be hot and served with a strong cup of English Breakfast tea.

name: American Breakfast (Grand Slam)
steps: Prepare pancake batter and cook on a griddle until bubbles form, then flip.
Fry the hash browns until they have a deep golden crust.
Cook bacon until very crispy.
Scramble the eggs with a touch of cream for fluffiness.
Stack the pancakes with butter in between layers.
Serve the eggs, bacon, and hash browns on the side with a generous pour of warm maple syrup.

name: Poached Eggs (Perfect Technique)
steps: Bring water to a gentle simmer (small bubbles). Add the vinegar (this helps the whites coagulate).
Crack each egg into a small ramekin first. This ensures no shell enters the water and allows a gentle drop.
Create a gentle whirlpool with a spoon. Drop the egg into the center.
Cook for 3 minutes for a very runny yolk, or 4 minutes for a jammy consistency.
Lift out with a slotted spoon and place on a paper towel for 5 seconds to remove excess water before serving on toast.

name: Scrambled Eggs (Silky French Style)
steps: Whisk the eggs in a cold saucepan (not a skillet). Add half of the cold butter.
Place over medium heat and stir constantly with a silicone spatula.
Cook for 30 seconds on the heat, then 30 seconds off the heat, while continuing to stir (the 'on-off' method).
Repeat until the eggs reach a custard-like consistency with very small curds.
Stir in the remaining cold butter and the heavy cream to stop the cooking immediately.
Serve warm garnished with minced chives.

name: International Breakfast
steps: Lightly warm the croissant in an oven (do not toast it hard).
Arrange the cold cuts and cheese slices on a serving board or plate.
Place the jam in a small dipping bowl.
Add the fresh fruit to the plate for color and acidity.
Serve with a hot café au lait and the orange juice. This is an assembly-focused meal emphasizing quality ingredients over cooking.

name: Cheese Omelet (Classic Fold)
steps: Whisk eggs with a pinch of salt until no white streaks remain.
Melt butter in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat.
Pour in eggs and use a spatula to move the cooked edges toward the center, letting raw egg flow underneath.
When the top is almost set but still slightly wet, sprinkle the cheese over one half.
Carefully fold the other half over the cheese. Cook for 1 more minute to melt.
Slide onto a plate and garnish with fresh parsley.

name: Asian Breakfast Plate (Congee & Sides)
steps: Boil the rice in the chicken broth for about 45-60 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the rice breaks down and creates a thick, creamy porridge (Congee).
Ladle the hot congee into a deep bowl.
Top with the shredded ginger and pickled mustard greens.
Slice the century egg or salted egg and place on top.
Serve with pieces of crispy fried dough (Youtiao) for dipping into the porridge.
Drizzle with a drop of sesame oil and white pepper.

name: Eggs Benedict
steps: Prepare the Hollandaise: Whisk 2 egg yolks and lemon juice in a glass bowl over a pot of simmering water (Bain-marie).
Slowly drizzle in the hot melted butter while whisking vigorously until the sauce is thick and doubled in volume. Season with cayenne and salt; keep warm.
Poach 2 eggs in acidulated water (water with vinegar) for 3 minutes until the whites are set but yolks are runny.
Toast the English muffin halves and lightly sear the ham in a skillet.
Assemble: Place ham on the muffins, top with a poached egg, and smother with a generous amount of Hollandaise sauce.
Garnish with fresh chives or paprika.

name: African Breakfast Plate (Akara & Pap)
steps: Akara: Blend the soaked/peeled beans with the onion and habanero using very little water to form a thick paste.
Whisk the paste with a wooden spoon for 5 minutes to incorporate air; this makes the bean cakes fluffy.
Deep fry spoonfuls of the paste in hot oil until golden brown and airy. Drain on paper towels.
Pap: Mix the corn starch (Ogi) with a little cold water to form a smooth paste. Pour boiling water over it while stirring constantly until it thickens into a smooth custard.
Serve the hot Akara balls on a plate alongside a bowl of creamy Pap sweetened with milk and sugar.

name: Breakfast Burrito
steps: Fry the chorizo and hash browns in a skillet until the meat is browned and potatoes are crispy.
Scramble the eggs in the same pan, incorporating the meat and potatoes.
Warm the tortilla until soft. Spread a line of salsa and cheese down the center.
Top with the egg mixture. Fold the sides in and roll tightly from the bottom.
Place the burrito seam-side down on the hot skillet for 1 minute to 'seal' the wrap and melt the cheese internally.

name: Chicken Soup (Homestyle Noodle)
steps: In a large pot, sear the chicken thighs skin-side down to render the fat. Remove and set aside.
Sauté the onion, celery, and carrots in the chicken fat until softened.
Add the chicken back to the pot and pour in the broth. Bring to a boil, then simmer on low for 30 minutes.
Remove the chicken, shred the meat from the bones, and return the meat to the pot.
Add the egg noodles and cook for 8 minutes until tender.
Finish with fresh parsley and season with salt and plenty of black pepper.

name: Vegetable Soup (Minestrone Style)
steps: Sauté diced onions, garlic, and carrots in olive oil.
Add the zucchini, green beans, and diced tomatoes. Season with oregano.
Pour in the vegetable broth and bring to a simmer.
Add the ditalini pasta and cook for 10 minutes.
Stir in the white beans and a handful of spinach at the very end.
Serve with a sprinkle of parmesan cheese and a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

name: Beef Soup (Beef & Barley)
steps: Brown the beef cubes in a large pot until deeply caramelized. Remove.
Sauté onions, carrots, and celery. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the beef back along with the broth, barley, and thyme.
Simmer on low for 1.5 hours until the beef is fork-tender.
Add the potatoes in the last 20 minutes of cooking so they don't turn mushy.
The barley will naturally thicken the soup into a rich, hearty stew-like consistency.

name: Noodle Soup (Asian Ginger Broth)
steps: Simmer the sliced ginger and smashed garlic in the broth for 10 minutes to infuse.
Discard the ginger pieces. Season the broth with soy sauce.
Add the noodles directly to the broth and cook until tender.
In the last 2 minutes, add the halved baby bok choy to the pot.
Turn off the heat and stir in the sesame oil.
Serve hot, garnished with sliced green onions and a soft-boiled egg if desired.

name: Ramen Soup (Shoyu Style)
steps: Heat the broth to a gentle simmer. Do not boil vigorously.
In a deep ramen bowl, place the Shoyu Tare base.
Boil the ramen noodles in a separate pot of water for 2 minutes. Drain well.
Pour the hot broth over the tare in the bowl and whisk slightly.
Add the noodles and straighten them with chopsticks.
Top with chashu pork, the halved egg, and the nori sheet. Serve immediately while the noodles are firm.

name: Pho Soup (Beef Phở)
steps: Parboil bones for 10 mins, drain and rinse (this ensures a clear broth).
Simmer bones with charred ginger, onion, and toasted spices for at least 3 hours. Skim impurities constantly.
Season the broth with fish sauce and rock sugar. Strain until perfectly clear.
Blanch rice noodles and place in a bowl.
Lay raw beef slices on top of the noodles. Pour boiling hot broth over them; the heat will cook the meat instantly.
Serve with a side of bean sprouts, lime, and Thai basil.

name: Creamy Soup (Potato Leek)
steps: Wash the leeks thoroughly to remove sand. Sauté in butter until soft but not browned.
Add the diced potatoes and broth. Simmer for 20 minutes until potatoes are falling apart.
Blend the soup until completely smooth using an immersion blender.
Stir in the heavy cream and heat through gently (do not boil).
Season with salt and white pepper. Serve hot or chilled (Vichyssoise style) garnished with chives.

name: Broth Soup (Consommé Style)
steps: Mix the cold ground beef, egg whites, and minced vegetables to create a 'raft'.
Whisk the raft into the cold stock in a pot.
Slowly bring to a simmer. As it heats, the raft will rise to the top, trapping all impurities.
Stop stirring once the raft has formed. Simmer gently for 45 minutes, allowing the liquid to bubble through a hole in the center of the raft.
Carefully ladle the crystal-clear liquid through a cheesecloth. The result should be a deep amber, fat-free, intense broth.

name: Roasted Tomato Soup
steps: Place tomatoes, onion, and the garlic head on a baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and salt.
Roast at 200°C for 35 minutes until charred and soft.
Squeeze the roasted garlic cloves out of their skins into a blender. Add the roasted vegetables and all pan juices.
Add fresh basil and vegetable broth. Blend until creamy.
Return to a pot to heat through. Serve with a grilled cheese sandwich.

name: Mushroom Soup (Cream of Mushroom)
steps: Sauté mushrooms in butter over high heat until they release their water and turn deep brown.
Sprinkle flour over the mushrooms and cook for 1 minute (making a roux).
Slowly pour in the broth while whisking to avoid lumps.
Add thyme and simmer for 15 minutes. Puree half of the soup in a blender for thickness while keeping the rest chunky.
Stir in the cream and season with salt and white pepper.

name: Seafood Soup (Bouillabaisse Style)
steps: Sauté sliced fennel, onions, and garlic in olive oil. Deglaze with white wine.
Add tomato puree, saffron, orange zest, and 1 Liter of fish stock. Simmer for 15 minutes.
Add the seafood in order: first the fish chunks, then the mussels and shrimp.
Cover and simmer for 5-7 minutes until the mussels open.
Serve in a wide bowl with a side of baguette spread with Rouille (garlic-saffron mayo).

name: Spicy Soup (Tom Yum Goong)
steps: Bring 3 cups of water to a boil with bruised lemongrass, galangal, and lime leaves.
Add the chili paste and mushrooms; simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the shrimp and cook until pink (about 2 minutes).
Turn off the heat (critical to preserve lime flavor).
Stir in the fish sauce, lime juice, and fresh chilies. Taste for a sharp sour-spicy balance.
Garnish with cilantro and serve piping hot.

name: Healthy Soup (Green Detox)
steps: Simmer the broccoli and grated ginger in vegetable broth for 8 minutes until tender.
Add the fresh spinach to the pot for the last 2 minutes only to keep it vibrant green.
Transfer the mixture to a high-speed blender. Add the lemon juice and a pinch of sea salt.
Blend on high until perfectly smooth and frothy.
Pour into a bowl and garnish with hemp seeds and a crack of black pepper. Designed for alkalinity and nutrient density.

name: Vegan Soup (Roasted Butternut)
steps: Toss squash cubes and onion with olive oil and roast at 200°C for 25 minutes until caramelized.
Place roasted vegetables into a pot with broth and sage. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove sage leaves and blend the soup until silky.
Stir in the coconut milk and maple syrup for a rich, velvety texture without dairy.
Season with nutmeg and salt. Serve hot with toasted seeds.

name: Street Soup (Mexican Corn Chowder)
steps: In a pot, sauté garlic and half of the corn in oil until the corn is slightly charred.
Add broth and smoked paprika. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Blend half of the soup and return it to the pot; this keeps it chunky while adding creaminess.
Stir in the remaining corn and the Greek yogurt.
Finish with fresh lime juice and serve in a cup, topped with chili powder and cilantro.

name: Traditional Soup (Avgolemono)
steps: Bring broth to a boil and cook the orzo until tender (about 10 mins). Add shredded chicken.
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs and lemon juice until frothy.
TEMPERING (Critical): Slowly whisk two ladles of the hot broth into the egg mixture. This prevents the eggs from scrambling when added to the soup.
Pour the tempered egg mixture back into the main pot while stirring constantly.
Cook on low for 2 minutes (do not boil). The soup will thicken into a silky, opaque yellow cream.

name: Classic Fries
steps: Peel and slice potatoes into uniform 1cm sticks. Soak in cold water for 30 minutes to remove starch.
Pat the sticks bone-dry with a towel (moisture causes soggy fries).
First Fry: Fry at 150°C for 5 minutes until soft but not brown. Drain and let cool completely.
Second Fry: Increase heat to 190°C. Fry for 2-3 minutes until deep golden and shatter-crisp.
Drain on a wire rack and salt immediately while hot.

name: Sweet Potato Fries
steps: Cut sweet potatoes into thin batons. Toss in a bowl with cornstarch until evenly coated.
Add olive oil and paprika; toss again. The cornstarch absorbs surface moisture for extra crunch.
Spread in a single layer on a parchment-lined sheet (do not crowd, or they will steam).
Bake at 220°C for 10 minutes, flip, and bake for 8-10 more minutes.
Let sit for 2 minutes out of the oven to firm up before serving.

name: Roasted Vegetables
steps: Halve the sprouts and slice carrots into thick rounds. Place on a baking sheet.
Drizzle with oil, balsamic, garlic powder, and fresh herbs. Toss well.
Roast at 200°C for 25 minutes. Every 10 minutes, give the tray a shake.
The vegetables are done when the edges are charred and the centers are tender.
Remove herb sprigs and serve as the perfect nutritious side.

name: Grilled Vegetables
steps: Slice the zucchini and eggplant into 1cm thick rounds. Cut the pepper into large flats.
Brush both sides of the vegetables generously with olive oil and salt.
Heat a grill pan over high heat. Grill the vegetables for 3-4 minutes per side until prominent char marks appear.
Arrange on a platter while hot.
Drizzle with cold balsamic glaze right before serving to add acidity and sweetness.

name: Garlic Bread
steps: Slice the bread in half lengthwise. In a bowl, mix butter, garlic, and oregano.
Spread the garlic butter heavily on both cut sides of the bread.
Sprinkle with parmesan cheese.
Broil at 220°C for 5-8 minutes until the edges are dark brown and the butter has soaked into the center.
Slice into 2-inch pieces and serve while the butter is still bubbling.

name: Side Salad
steps: Spin the lettuce until dry. Combine with cucumber in a chilled bowl.
Add the dressing only right before serving to avoid wilting.
Toss gently with two spoons.
Designed as a palate cleanser to accompany heavy mains.

name: Rice Side (Herbed Basmati)
steps: Boil rice in 1.5 cups of water until fluffy.
Stir in a knob of butter and fresh parsley.
A simple, neutral side for stews and grilled meats.

name: Beans Side (Savory Black Beans)
steps: Simmer the beans in their own liquid with spices for 10 minutes.
Mash a small portion of the beans with a fork to thicken the sauce.
Garnish with cilantro. Perfect side for rice bowls or tacos.

name: Corn Side (Creamed Corn)
steps: Sauté corn in butter for 5 minutes until sweet.
Pour in cream and nutmeg. Simmer until the cream reduces by half and coats the corn.
Season with salt and lots of black pepper. A classic comfort side dish.

name: Snack Plate (Mezze Spread)
steps: Arrange the cold components (feta, olives, cucumber) in distinct sections on a plate.
Place the hummus in a small central ramekin with a drizzle of oil.
Warm the pita wedges and place them around the edges.
Designed for sharing or a high-protein afternoon snack.

name: Protein Snack (Power Duo)
steps: Hard boil the egg for exactly 10 minutes. Shock in ice water to make peeling easy.
Place the peeled egg in a portable container.
Add the almonds and turkey jerky.
High protein and low carb snack to keep blood sugar stable throughout the day.

name: Healthy Snack
steps: Core and slice the apple into uniform wedges. Dip them briefly in lemon water to prevent browning if not eating immediately.
Place the apple wedges on a plate.
Drizzle or spread a teaspoon of peanut butter over each slice.
Sprinkle with hemp seeds for added protein and Omega-3s, and finish with a few dark chocolate chips for a healthy antioxidant boost.

name: Fruit Snack
steps: Peel the mango and slice the flesh into long, thick spears.
Arrange the spears in a cup or on a small plate.
Squeeze fresh lime juice over the mango to activate the flavors.
Dust generously with Tajín (chili-lime salt). The contrast between the sweet mango and the spicy, salty seasoning is the hallmark of this traditional snack.

name: Veggie Snack
steps: Wash and dry the celery and carrots. Cut them into 4-inch sticks.
Fill the hollow center of the celery stalks with hummus or light cream cheese.
Top the celery with raisins (classic 'Ants on a Log').
Serve alongside the carrot sticks for a high-fiber, low-glycemic snack that provides sustained energy.

name: Dips and Sides
steps: Arrange three small ramekins on a large platter.
Fill each with one of the dips (chickpea, yogurt-cucumber, and roasted eggplant).
Drizzle the hummus and baba ganoush with extra virgin olive oil and a dash of paprika.
Place the raw vegetable sticks and warm pita bread around the dips.
This 'trio' approach ensures a variety of probiotics, healthy fats, and vitamins in one snack.

name: Loaded Fries
steps: Double-fry the potatoes at 150°C and then 190°C to ensure the interior is fluffy and the exterior can support the heavy toppings.
Drain the fries and salt them while piping hot.
Place the fries in a shallow bowl or on a small baking sheet.
Pour hot cheddar cheese sauce evenly over the fries.
Top with the crispy bacon, sliced jalapeños, and green onions.
Add a final dollop of cold sour cream in the center to balance the heat. Serve with a fork.

name: Quick Snack
steps: Place the rice cake on a plate. Peel the string cheese into fine threads and lay them across the rice cake.
Assemble the nuts and dried apricots on the side.
This snack is designed for the '3-minute assembly' rule, providing healthy fats, protein, and natural sugars to prevent afternoon energy crashes.

name: Street Snack (Elote en Vaso)
steps: Steam or boil the corn kernels until hot and tender.
Fill a tall glass or cup halfway with corn.
Add half of the mayonnaise and a squeeze of lime; stir well to coat the warm corn.
Fill the rest of the cup with corn.
Top with the remaining mayo, a heavy dusting of Cotija cheese, and chili powder.
Serve with a spoon. This 'Corn in a Cup' style is the most popular way to eat Elote as a handheld street snack.

name: Chocolate Cake
steps: Preheat oven to 175°C (350°F). Grease and flour two 8-inch cake pans.
Whisk dry ingredients (flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda) in a large bowl.
Add eggs, buttermilk, and oil; beat until smooth.
SECRET STEP: Stir in the hot coffee by hand. The heat 'blooms' the cocoa powder for a deeper chocolate flavor, and the thin batter ensures a moist crumb.
Bake for 30-35 minutes. Cool completely in the pans before frosting with chocolate ganache.

name: Vanilla Cake
steps: Creaming Method: Beat softened butter and sugar for 5 minutes until very pale and fluffy. This incorporates air for a light cake.
Add egg whites one at a time, beating well after each. Stir in the vanilla bean paste.
Alternate adding the flour and the milk, starting and ending with flour.
Divide batter between pans and bake at 180°C (350°F) for 25-30 minutes.
The use of egg whites only and cake flour results in a pure white, delicate sponge.

name: Ice Cream (Vanilla Bean Churn)
steps: Heat milk, half the sugar, and the vanilla bean/seeds in a pot until just simmering.
Whisk egg yolks and remaining sugar in a bowl until pale yellow.
TEMPERING: Slowly pour a ladle of hot milk into the yolks while whisking constantly, then return the entire mix to the pot.
Cook over low heat until the custard coats the back of a spoon (82°C). Do not boil.
Stir in the heavy cream. Chill the base in the fridge for at least 4 hours (overnight is best for texture).
Churn in an ice cream maker for 20 minutes, then freeze for 2 hours to 'ripen'.

name: Fruit Dessert (Apple Tarte Tatin)
steps: In an oven-proof skillet, melt butter and sugar over medium heat until a deep amber caramel forms.
Carefully arrange apple halves tightly in the skillet, cut-side up.
Cook on the stovetop for 15 minutes to soften the apples in the caramel.
Drape the puff pastry over the apples, tucking the edges down into the skillet.
Bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until the pastry is dark golden.
IMPORTANT: Let rest for 5 minutes, then invert onto a plate while the caramel is still liquid but starting to set. Serve warm with vanilla ice cream.

name: Cupcake (Red Velvet)
steps: Cream butter and sugar. Add the egg and the food coloring mixed with cocoa powder.
Add buttermilk and flour in alternating batches.
FINAL STEP: Mix baking soda and vinegar in a small cup (it will fizz) and immediately fold into the batter. This provides the chemical lift and traditional tang.
Bake at 175°C for 18-22 minutes.
Cool completely before piping with thick cream cheese frosting.

name: Pastry (Palmiers / Elephant Ears)
steps: Spread sugar and cinnamon heavily on your work surface. Lay the cold puff pastry on top.
Sprinkle more sugar on top of the pastry. Roll with a pin to press the sugar into the dough.
Fold the left and right sides toward the center until they meet in the middle, then fold one half over the other (like a book).
Slice into 1cm thick pieces. Place on a baking sheet, cut-side up.
Bake at 200°C for 12-15 minutes, flipping halfway through to caramelize the sugar on both sides.
The result should be 'shatter-crisp' and deeply caramelized.

name: Donuts (Classic Glazed)
steps: Knead a brioche-style dough (flour, yeast, milk, yolks, butter) for 10 minutes until elastic. Let rise for 1 hour.
Roll dough to 1.5cm thickness and cut out circles with a center hole.
SECOND RISE: Let the cut donuts rest for 45 minutes until they look puffy and light. This is the key to the 'white ring' in professional donuts.
Fry at 175°C for 90 seconds per side.
Dip into warm vanilla glaze immediately while the donut is still hot to ensure a thin, crackly shell.

name: Chocolate Dessert (Molten Lava Cake)
steps: Preheat oven to 220°C (425°F). Butter and cocoa powder the inside of two 6oz ramekins.
Melt chocolate and butter together until smooth.
In a bowl, whisk eggs, yolks, and sugar until thick and pale.
Fold the chocolate into the eggs, then gently stir in the flour.
Bake for exactly 11-13 minutes. The edges should be firm but the center should still jiggle slightly.
Let sit for 1 minute, then invert onto a plate. The center will flow out like lava when cut.

name: Layered Dessert (Strawberry Trifle)
steps: Prepare the jelly according to instructions and set it in the fridge until firm.
Place the sponge cake cubes at the bottom of a large glass trifle bowl and drizzle with sherry or juice.
Slice strawberries and press them against the glass sides for a decorative look.
Layer the set jelly over the cake, followed by a thick layer of cold vanilla custard.
Whip the cream to soft peaks and spread it as the final top layer.
Chill for at least 4 hours before serving to allow the layers to settle and the sponge to soften.

name: Baklava (Honey & Pistachio)
steps: Layer 10 sheets of phyllo in a buttered pan, brushing each layer generously with melted butter.
Spread half of the chopped nut mixture evenly over the pastry.
Add 5 more layers of buttered phyllo, the rest of the nuts, and finish with 10 more layers of phyllo.
Cut the baklava into diamond shapes with a very sharp knife BEFORE baking.
Bake at 165°C for 50 minutes until deep golden and crispy.
CRITICAL STEP: Immediately pour COLD honey-sugar syrup over the boiling HOT baklava. The 'sizzle' ensures the syrup is absorbed without making the pastry soggy.

name: Rice Pudding (Arroz con Leche)
steps: Simmer the rice in 2 cups of water with the cinnamon stick and lemon peel for 15 minutes until water is absorbed.
Slowly pour in the whole milk and simmer on low, stirring constantly to release the rice starch.
Once the mixture is creamy (about 25 mins), stir in the condensed milk for sweetness and extra richness.
Cook for another 5 minutes, then remove from heat and discard the peel and cinnamon stick.
Serve warm or chilled, topped with a heavy dusting of ground cinnamon.

name: Custard (Crème Brûlée)
steps: Heat cream and vanilla seeds in a pot until just simmering.
Whisk egg yolks and sugar until pale. Temper the eggs by slowly whisking in the hot cream.
Strain the custard and pour into individual ramekins.
Bake in a water bath (bain-marie) at 150°C for 35-40 minutes until set but still slightly wobbly in the center.
Chill for at least 4 hours. Before serving, sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on top.
Use a kitchen torch to caramelize the sugar into a hard, glass-like crust.

name: Pancake Dessert (Crêpes Suzette)
steps: In a wide skillet, melt butter and sugar until it starts to caramelize.
Add orange juice and zest; simmer until the sauce reduces and thickens.
Fold the crepes into triangles and place them in the bubbling orange sauce.
Pour the liqueur over the crepes and carefully ignite with a long match (flambé).
Baste the crepes with the flaming sauce until the alcohol evaporates. Serve immediately.

name: Waffle Dessert (Liège style)
steps: Fold the pearl sugar into the risen yeast dough by hand. The sugar pearls should be evenly distributed.
Heat a heavy-duty waffle iron to medium-high.
Place a ball of dough in the center. The pearl sugar will melt and caramelize on the surface, creating a crunchy glaze.
Cook for 4 minutes until dark golden brown.
Serve warm topped with whipped cream and a rich dark chocolate drizzle.

name: Healthy Dessert (Chia Berry Pudding)
steps: Whisk chia seeds, coconut milk, and maple syrup in a bowl.
Let sit for 10 minutes, then whisk again to prevent the seeds from clumping at the bottom.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or overnight).
Layer the set pudding in a glass with fresh mashed raspberries.
Top with a few whole berries and a mint leaf.

name: Vegan Dessert (Chocolate Avocado Mousse)
steps: Place the avocado flesh into a food processor.
Add cocoa powder, maple syrup, vanilla, and salt.
Process until the mixture is completely smooth and no green bits remain.
Chill for 1 hour to allow the texture to firm up and the flavors to meld.
Serve with a sprinkle of sea salt or fresh berries.

name: Keto Dessert (Almond Flour Brownies)
steps: Whisk eggs and erythritol until well combined. Stir in the melted butter.
Sift in the almond flour and cocoa powder.
Fold gently until a thick batter forms.
Bake in an 8x8 pan at 175°C for 20 minutes.
Let cool completely before slicing (Keto baked goods are very fragile while warm).

name: High Protein Dessert (Protein Cheesecake)
steps: Press the oat flour mixed with a little water into the bottom of a springform pan.
Blend yogurt, protein powder, egg whites, and stevia until smooth.
Pour over the crust and bake at 160°C for 45 minutes.
Turn off the oven and leave the door slightly ajar for 1 hour to prevent the top from cracking.
Chill for 6 hours before serving.

name: Middle Eastern Dessert (Kunafa)
steps: Finely shred the kataifi pastry and mix thoroughly with melted ghee.
Press half the pastry into a round baking pan.
Add a thick layer of desalted cheese in the center.
Top with the remaining pastry and bake at 190°C until the top is very crispy and golden.
Pour hot syrup over the hot kunafa immediately.
Garnish with ground pistachios and serve warm while the cheese is stretchy.

name: Asian Dessert (Mango Pomelo Sago)
steps: Boil sago for 10 minutes until translucent, then rinse in cold water.
Blend mango flesh with coconut milk and a little evaporated milk until smooth.
Stir the cooked sago into the mango mixture.
Chill for 2 hours.
Serve in bowls topped with more fresh mango cubes and the bitter-sweet pomelo segments.

name: Latin Dessert (Tres Leches Cake)
steps: Bake a light sponge cake and let it cool.
Use a fork to poke holes all over the surface of the cake.
Whisk the three milks (evaporated, condensed, cream) together.
Pour the milk mixture slowly over the cake. The holes allow it to soak through to the center.
Refrigerate overnight—this is the most important step for the correct texture.
Top with whipped cream and a sprinkle of cinnamon before serving.

name: Quick Dessert (Affogato)
steps: Place two scoops of premium vanilla gelato in a chilled glass.
Brew a fresh double shot of espresso.
Immediately pour the hot coffee over the cold gelato.
Top with crushed amaretti for texture and serve with a small spoon before it fully melts.

name: Easy Dessert (Fruit Galette)
steps: Roll out the pie crust into a rough circle on a baking sheet.
Toss the sliced fruit with sugar and cornstarch.
Pile the fruit in the center of the dough, leaving a 2-inch border.
Fold the edges of the dough over the fruit, pleating as you go to create a rustic border.
Brush the dough with egg wash and bake at 200°C for 25 minutes until golden.
Perfectly 'easy' because it requires no pie pan or precise crimping.

name: Traditional Dessert (Sticky Toffee Pudding)
steps: Soak dates in boiling water with baking soda until mushy, then blend into a paste.
Cream butter and sugar; add eggs and the date paste. Fold in flour.
Bake in individual molds at 180°C for 30 minutes.
Make the toffee sauce by boiling butter, brown sugar, and cream until dark and thick.
Poke holes in the warm cakes and pour half the sauce over them. Broil for 2 minutes to caramelize.
Serve with the remaining hot sauce and vanilla custard.

name: Street Food Dessert (Loukoumades)
steps: Allow the yeast dough to rise until it is bubbly and airy.
Heat oil to 180°C. Squeeze small balls of dough through your thumb and forefinger into the oil.
Fry until the 'donuts' are deep golden and float to the top.
Drain and immediately toss in warm honey syrup.
Sprinkle with lots of cinnamon and crushed walnuts. Eat with a toothpick.

name: Bistro Dessert (Profiteroles)
steps: Bake small balls of choux pastry at 200°C until puffed and hollow. Let them cool completely.
Slice the shells in half horizontally.
Place a large scoop of vanilla ice cream in the bottom half of each shell.
Place the top half back on.
Assemble three on a plate and pour piping hot chocolate sauce over the top so it melts the ice cream slightly.
Finish with toasted almonds.

name: Gourmet Dessert (Chocolate Soufflé)
steps: CRITICAL: Butter the ramekins with upward strokes and coat with sugar/cocoa. This helps the soufflé climb straight up.
Whisk egg whites and sugar to stiff, glossy peaks.
Fold 1/3 of the whites into the melted chocolate to lighten it.
Gently fold in the remaining whites, being careful not to deflate the air.
Fill ramekins to the brim and level off with a knife.
Bake at 190°C for 12-15 minutes. Serve immediately before the air cools and it collapses.

name: Fruit Tart (Tarte aux Fruits)
steps: Blind bake the tart shell until golden and crisp. Let cool.
Fill the shell with cold, silky pastry cream and smooth the surface.
Arrange the fruit in concentric circles (kiwi, strawberries, blueberries).
Brush the fruit with warm apricot glaze to provide a professional shine and prevent the fruit from drying out.
Chill for 1 hour before slicing.

name: Chocolate Mousse (Classic Dark)
steps: Melt the chocolate over a bain-marie. Let it cool to room temperature.
Whip the cream to soft peaks.
Whip egg whites and sugar to stiff peaks.
Fold the whipped cream into the chocolate, then gently fold in the egg whites.
The result should be light, airy, and full of bubbles.
Chill for at least 4 hours to set the structure.

name: Panna Cotta (Vanilla Bean)
steps: Soak gelatin sheets in cold water for 5 minutes (blooming).
Heat cream, sugar, and vanilla in a pot until the sugar dissolves. Do not boil.
Squeeze the water out of the gelatin and stir into the warm cream until dissolved.
Pour into molds and refrigerate for 6 hours.
To unmold, dip the bottom of the mold in hot water for 5 seconds.
Serve with a bright raspberry coulis to cut through the richness.

name: Tiramisu (Authentic Venetian)
steps: Whisk yolks and sugar over a pot of simmering water (Sabayon) until thick and pale. Let cool.
Gently fold the mascarpone into the egg mixture until smooth.
Dip each ladyfinger into the coffee-Marsala mix for only 1 second per side (do not soak).
Layer dipped cookies in a dish, cover with half the cream, and repeat.
Refrigerate for at least 12 hours to allow the coffee to migrate and the cream to set.
Dust heavily with cocoa powder right before serving.

name: Cheesecake (New York Style)
steps: Beat cream cheese and sugar until perfectly smooth. Add eggs one at a time on low speed.
Fold in sour cream and vanilla.
Wrap the outside of the springform pan in double foil to make it waterproof.
Bake in a water bath at 160°C for 90 minutes.
Leave in the oven with the door closed for 2 hours after turning it off (this prevents cracks).
Chill for 24 hours for the perfect dense, creamy texture.

name: Brownie (Sea Salt Fudgy)
steps: Melt chocolate and butter. Whisk sugar and eggs for 5 minutes until very frothy (creates the crackly top).
Fold the chocolate into the eggs.
Gently stir in flour and cocoa powder.
Bake at 175°C for 25 minutes. The center should still be slightly moist.
Sprinkle flaky sea salt over the top while hot. Let cool completely before cutting into squares.

name: Cookies (White Chocolate Macadamia)
steps: Brown the butter in a pan until it smells nutty and has brown specks; let cool.
Cream the browned butter with sugar and egg.
Fold in the nuts and white chocolate.
Chill the dough balls in the fridge for 24 hours (essential for flavor depth and texture).
Bake at 180°C for 10-12 minutes until the edges are just barely golden.

name: Gourmet Finale (French Macarons)
steps: Sift almond flour and powdered sugar three times to ensure zero lumps.
Whisk egg whites to stiff peaks, adding granulated sugar slowly.
MACARONAGE: Fold the dry mix into the whites until the batter flows like 'slow lava'.
Pipe 3cm circles and TAP THE TRAY hard on the counter to release air bubbles.
Let sit for 45 minutes until a skin forms (critical for the 'feet').
Bake at 150°C for 15 minutes. Sandwich with ganache once cooled.
